blocks.text: have the ability to bring a warrior out of a berserk rage. The chances for success are equal to an Easy Harmony Rune affinity roll for a pacifist of Devotee rank, and Standard roll for a pacifist of Initiate rank.

blocks.name: Bind Ghost
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Years), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death glyph, Spirit glyph]
blocks.text: Other than the above changes, use the default Bind Ghost miracle (RuneQuest page 266).

blocks.name: Bind Ship
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Ranged (Kilometres), Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Resist (Seamanship), Runes [Water glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: When cast upon a boat or ship, this spell slows the Movement Rate of the vessel at the rate of 1 knot per minute until it is completely stopped. When the spell expires, the ship may begin moving normally, though it must accelerate from a stop. The maximum size of the vessels which can be affected depends on the Intensity of the spell as indicated by the Bind Ship table. If the ship has sails, they are strained by this deceleration. The chance of breaking or damaging the ship's rigging is equal to the Wind STR or less on 1D100 (see RuneQuest, page 130), rolled when the ship comes to a complete halt. Bind Ship is resisted by the Seamanship skill of whoever is captaining the vessel.

blocks.name: Black Breath
blocks.stat_line: Area (Half-Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Special), Runes [Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: Under the effects of this spell, the caster breathes out a shadowy mist of writhing tendrils which will poison anyone who inhales them. Those that fail to hold their breath are subject to a poison with a Potency equal to 10 times the spell's Intensity. The caster is immune to the poison's effects.

blocks.heading: Black Breath Poison
blocks.text: Magical tendrils of dark shadow which freeze those that breathe them with deadly cold.

details.effect: Failing to resist the potency of the poison causes the victim to collapse from uncontrollable shivering, unable to control their physical movements. After collapsing, the Hit Location in which resides their respiratory system suffers terrible burns from the intense cold, taking 1 point of maiming damage to the region for every round they are exposed to the Black Breath.
details.antidote_cure: Shivering induced paralysis wears off 1d3 minutes after the victim leaves the spell area. Maiming damage however cannot be healed save by spells like Rejuvenate.

blocks.name: Blast Earth
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Months), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death glyph, Earth glyph]
blocks.text: When cast upon an area of land, Blast Earth scours and disrupts the soil, so that no plants within the area blasted may flower or bear fruit until the spell ends.

blocks.name: Bless Animals
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Months), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast glyph, Fertility glyph]
blocks.text: This fertility spell increases the calving potential for each beast within the initial area of effect. Each birth produces a healthy calf or, rarely, twins. Nine of ten calves born to a beast influenced by this spell are female. This spell is usually cast only on the cult's High Holy Day of the year, so that it aligns with the mating period of the cult's animals. Thus it affects the offspring produced in the following calving season.

blocks.name: Bless Crops
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Months), Rank Initiate, Runes [Earth glyph, Plant glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Bless Crops (RuneQuest page 266), with the above changes.

blocks.name: Bless Grave (Lay to Rest)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death glyph, Spirit glyph]
blocks.text: Once this ritual has been completed, it ensures the soul of a dead person passes into the afterlife smoothly and does not return as a bad ghost. Neither can their soul be summoned for questioning, bound as a sapient undead or brought back to life, unless the magic is performed by a worshipper of the same cult that cast the Bless Grave spell.

blocks.name: Bless Forge
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Days), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire glyph, Stasis glyph]
blocks.text: Bless Forge allows a smith to imbue tools, weapons and armour with greater than usual properties. Only a single project can be worked on whilst the magic lasts, but it increases the Task Rounds (RuneQuest page 100) invested to a number equal to the Intensity of the spell. Thus an Intensity 7 Bless Forge spell would permit the smith seven Task Rounds to fashion the item; which in all probability, will allow it to receive several Enhancements (RuneQuest page 101).

blocks.name: Bless Home
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Years), Rank Initiate, Runes [Protection-like glyph, Harmony glyph]
blocks.text: This ritual is performed over a home and affects it and the family who dwells therein. Family members within the blessed home are warded against malicious spells and hostile spirits which cross its walls, as if protected by a combined Countermagic and Spirit Shield of equal Intensity. The enchantment ends if ever the family hearth is shattered.

blocks.name: Bless Thunderstone
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Years), Rank Initiate, Runes [Storm glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: Thunderstones are potent magical weapons formed from palm sized flints or similar stones. Once enchanted, the thunderstone remains quiescent until thrown, slung or struck against a target, at which point it shatters in a shower of sparks. For each two full points of Intensity, the stone does 1d6 Hit Points damage and destroys 1d6 Magic Points of a struck target. Armour protects normally against the Hit Point damage done by the stone, and likewise the Magic Point drain is reduced by the Magnitude of magic protective spells. The caster may only regain the Magic Points invested in the stone once it has shattered or the spell ends naturally.

blocks.name: Bless Woad
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Years), Rank Initiate, Runes [Storm glyph, Stasis glyph]
blocks.text: Enchants a pot of woad sufficient to coat the skin of a single user, granting them protection against wounds and magic. The woad grants its wearer Countermagic and Armour Points equal to its Intensity. Each day that the woad is worn, it declines in potency by 1 point. If the user ever puts on armour or clothing, the woad immediately loses all its magic power. Magic Points invested into a pot of blessed woad do not return until the woad is used or the enchantment expires.

blocks.name: Bless Worshippers
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Years), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph, Law-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be stacked with another theistic spell known by the caster. For each point of Intensity in Bless Worshippers, the other spell takes effect on an additional target, who must be a worshipper of the same cult. The spell stacked with Bless Worshippers is thereafter lost until the priest returns to a cult shrine or temple to pray for its return.

blocks.name: Blinding
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Stasis glyph]
blocks.text: This spell blinds the target entity if they fail to resist. Unless they possess other senses to compensate, any skill requiring sight becomes Herculean difficulty.

blocks.name: Blood Feast
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Special), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Disorder glyph]
blocks.text: Blood Feast requires the caster to ritually prepare a meat stew in a cauldron, using the body organs of several creatures. After the cooking has been completed, all cult members participating in the feast consume the stew and may donate a Magic Point to the ritual. The result is a spectre, an artificial undead spirit, its appearance conjoined from the hellish mix of the species and body parts in the stew. Use the base

Allows the spectre free will, the ability to act without guidance.
Allows the spectre to manifest into the material world (Wraith Form).
Allows the spectre to consume the Magic Points of things it kills, to replenish its own.
Allows the spectre to influence emotions (Miasma).
Allows the spectre to roam freely away from its place of creation.
Allows the spectre magical armour equal to its Intensity.
Allows the spectre to cast the Basic Magic known to the cult (Cast Magic).

blocks.name: Blood Feast
blocks.text: Once created, the POW and subsequent Intensity of the spectre is based on the total number of Magic Points invested into its creation. The spectre does not recover Magic Points naturally, but must have them donated by the caster, or drained from victims if guts were included in its creation. The spectre persists for as long as the caster continues to maintain the Blood Feast from their Devotional Pool. In addition, every cult member who donated a Magic Point into the Blood Feast has their total Magic Points reduced by 1 until the spell ends.

blocks.name: Breathe Air
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Initiate, Runes [Storm glyph, Water glyph]
blocks.text: As per the Breathe Water spell, but instead allows aquatic water breathers to emerge above the surface and survive.

blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Initiate, Runes [Storm glyph, Water glyph]

blocks.name: Bump in the Night
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Darkness glyph, Disorder glyph]
blocks.text: Creates disconcerting sounds capable of unsettling listeners, but only if cast at night or underground. Anyone who fails to resist becomes subject to the effects of a Demoralise spell.

blocks.name: Buoyancy
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Special), Runes [Water glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: Each point of Intensity lifts 5 SIZ of creature, or if an inanimate object, its totalled corresponding mass on the Creature Size Equivalency table (RuneQuest page 327), to just below the surface of the water. Heavier objects will not float, though they will sink more slowly than if the spell had not been cast. Lighter objects will be raised to be about halfway out of the water, bobbing about. Unwilling creatures targeted by the spell may attempt a Swim roll to resist.

blocks.name: Butterflight
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Rank Priest, Runes [Air glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: Butterflight grows huge colourful wings from the caster's back, enabling them to fly at a Movement Rate of 8. The wings have no inherent Armour Points, but the fluttering imposes a penalty of Formidable on attempts to hit the user when aloft. Hit Points are equal to the caster's arms, and if damaged they use the Winged Biped Hit Location table (RuneQuest page 443).

blocks.name: Call Barracuda
blocks.stat_line: Area (Kilometres), Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Runes [Water glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes giant barracuda within range to cease whatever they are doing and race to the aid of the caster. Assuming any are present, the number of barracuda summoned equals the Intensity of the spell. Treat the monstrous fish as average sharks (RuneQuest page 384).

blocks.name: Call Founder
blocks.text: See Summon Founder (page 115).

blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Days), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Water glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: This ceremonial spell summons a deep-sea creature or group of creatures. It is cast by a group of worshipers, led by

blocks.name: Call Monster
blocks.text: a priest. If the casting is successful, each participant in the ritual may expend as many Magic Points from their Devotional Pool as desired towards the spell; or a single Magic Point if they are only lay members. The total of these accumulated Magic Points must exceed the combined Characteristic scores (STR, CON, SIZ, INT, POW and DEX), of the monster or monsters being summoned, otherwise the ritual fails. Once completed, the creatures take 1d100 hours to arrive and are not under the control of the summoners, unless a separate Command spell is cast to do so. Sapient monsters may need to be bargained with for their services. Magic Points dedicated to this spell only begin to return once the duration ends or the monsters are slain.

blocks.name: Cancel Light
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Months), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: Creates a hemispherical zone within which all magical light is dismissed if the Intensity of the Cancel Light is equal or greater than the light spell.

blocks.name: Carapace
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast glyph, Earth glyph]
blocks.text: This spell changes the recipient's skin to hard chitin, replacing the natural Armour Points of his torso to a value equal to the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Carry (Specific Disease)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [Disease/Chaos-like glyph, Death glyph]
blocks.text: This spell makes the target a carrier of one specific disease, exposing anyone they physically contact to the disease. It also causes the target to become permanently immune to the effects of that specified disease.

blocks.name: Catseye
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph, Beast glyph]
blocks.text: This spell affects the eyes of the recipient so that they can see by any amount of available light. If there is a complete absence of illumination, vision is impossible; however, if light from a single dim spark exists, they see normally, up to the maximum range of the magic. Eyes under this spell reflect light as do a cat's.

blocks.name: Cause (Specific Disease)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Disease/Chaos-like glyph, Death glyph]
blocks.text: If the target fails to resist, they contract a dangerous disease of a type known to the caster. The Intensity of the spell limits which disease condition it inflicts. Use the Disease Condition Cost table to reflect the spell's effect. Multiple conditions may be purchased if enough Intensity is available. Onset time and frequency of attacks is based upon the victim's resistance roll: on a Critical, the disease takes a day before manifesting, on a Success an hour, on a Failure a minute and on a Fumble it starts instantly. The inflicted disease continues until cured or the target eventually passes away.

blocks.name: Cause Plague
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Rank Priest, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Disease/Chaos-like glyph, Death glyph]
blocks.text: At the expense of their own life, the caster collapses into a putrid puddle of highly contagious, disease riddled slime. Anyone within the immediate area of effect is splattered by transformation and must resist the magic, but their Endurance roll is at a Hard penalty. Those that fail are both infected with the chosen disease (see Cause Disease) and also transmit it to anyone they approach within arm's reach whether physical contact is made or not. New victims must resist the disease at a Potency equal to the deceased caster's Devotion skill, or also become infected and carry it onwards. Even if the plague is contained or kills off the entire local population, the original spot the caster died remains a source for the disease until purified in some manner.

blocks.name: Chameleon
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Initiate, Runes [Disorder glyph, Plant-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Chameleon spell (RuneQuest page 266).

blocks.name: Chaos Feature
blocks.text: This ritual takes 12 hours and permanently gives the target a chaos feature. If unwilling, the recipient must try to resist the gift. If affected by the foul magic roll on the Chaotic Feature table (see Appendix C starting on page 209). For each chaotic feature an individual accepts, there is a 10% cumulative chance of turning into a broo. This transformation may either be resisted nor altered, and is permanent once it takes effect. The probability for the transformation is rolled immediately upon the assumption of each chaotic feature.

blocks.name: Chaos Gift
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Chaos glyph, Moon glyph]
blocks.text: For the duration of the spell, the caster gains a random chaos feature from the Chaotic Features table. If gained via the Moon Rune, this spell is subject to cyclical effects. Once this spell has been used, the recipient always registers as chaos-tainted by appropriate detection magics, abilities or spirits. On a critical casting success, the user can choose which chaotic feature they wish to obtain.

blocks.name: Chaos Spawn
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Chaos glyph]
blocks.text: This spell summons a wailing, moaning void appearing in mid-air. That flung into or otherwise entering this void is irrevocably lost, but 1d3 Turns later, the void leaves a random chaos creature in its stead. Roll 1D10 to determine what it is. All creatures have a chance to hit equal to the Devotion skill of the caster. Neither the POW nor SIZ of the arrival may be greater than the respective POW or SIZ of the sacrifice, unless no sacrifice is made, when a random gorp appears. At the end of the spell, the wailing void reappears, envelopes the summoned creature, then vanishes for good after another 1d3 Turns.

blocks.title: Chaos Spawn
blocks.notes: The table is degraded but legible after zooming; 'Glarg' is visually likely.

blocks.name: Charisma
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Runes [Movement glyph, Disorder glyph]
blocks.text: Use the Enthrall spell (RuneQuest page 269).

blocks.name: Chastise
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Runes Any
blocks.text: This spell punishes enemies or those that transgress cult tenets, as per the default Corruption miracle (RuneQuest page 267).

blocks.name: Chill
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Rounds), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Darkness glyph, Water glyph]
blocks.text: Chill causes the target of the spell to suffer the effects of freezing cold in their very bones. If not resisted, at the end of each Round the spell inflicts 1d3 internal damage to every Hit Location of the victim, ignoring natural and worn armour. Creatures with the Cold Blooded ability who succumb to this spell immediately become torpid.

blocks.name: Chomping
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Rounds), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Disease/Chaos-like glyph, Death glyph, Beast glyph]
blocks.text: Chomping causes the recipient's jaws and teeth enlarge and sharpen. For each two full points of Intensity, the damage dice and weapon size of the target's bite attack are raised by one step.

blocks.name: City Harmony
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Harmony glyph, Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell affects everyone in the area of effect and keeps them from getting excited or violent if they fail to resist. It counteracts emotion-affecting spells (such as Demoralize and Fanaticism) with a Magnitude weaker than that of the City Harmony spell.

blocks.name: Clairvoyance/Clairaudience
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Storm glyph, Fire/Sun glyph, Beast glyph]
blocks.text: Clairvoyance gives the caster the ability to know what is happening in an area which has been previously studied for at least 15 minutes. Depending on their runic association, the user has full audio or visual perception (or both), as if

uncertain_readings: Chaos Feature contains a sentence that appears to read 'may either be resisted nor altered'; this is likely a proofing or scan artifact, but it is preserved with minimal correction.
'Glarg' in the Chaos Spawn table is visually likely but should be verified against a cleaner source.
Clairvoyance/Clairaudience continues on the next page.
corrections_applied: Corrected table rows using zoomed rendered image.
Normalised broken line spacing and spell headings.

blocks.name: Clairvoyance/Clairaudience
blocks.text: physically present in the area. Until the spell ends, the caster is in full trance, completely insensible to their surroundings.

blocks.name: Claws
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Rounds), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell turns one of the caster's arms, or tail if possessing one, into a large scorpion-like pincer. The claw does 1d8 damage plus any damage modifier, and has a weapon size upon the Intensity of the spell: 1-2 Small, 3-4 Medium, 5-6 Large, 7-8 Huge, 9-10 Enormous. If the caster lacks claw as part of their known Weapon Styles, they can use the Unarmed skill instead.

blocks.name: Clever Tongue
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Clever Tongue increases the eloquence of the recipient so that they are a delight to listen to. Any failed roll of a verbal skill, such as Commerce, Influence, Oratory and so on, is treated as a success instead. Fumbles, normal successes and critical successes remain unaffected.

blocks.name: Cloud Call
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Use the default Cloud Call miracle (RuneQuest page 267).

blocks.name: Cloud Clear
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Use the default Clear Skies miracle (RuneQuest page 267).

blocks.name: Coerce
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Rank Priest, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Mastery-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to force a number of individuals equal to the spell's Intensity to obey his will. It automatically succeeds against targets wearing enchanted slave shackles, but other victims' Willpower must be overcome. It takes a full round of concentration to issue new commands; this is done through a form of Mindlink, and does not require the caster to speak aloud or the victim to share a common language. The caster cannot make victims take any action that will directly cause injury; doing so immediately ends the spell's effect. The spell does not function against cult members who hold superior or equal status to the caster.

blocks.name: Coin Wheel
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph, Earth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a block of gold, causing a coin to separate from the rest of the mass. Called a wheel in common Gloranthan parlance, these coins are commonly worth twice their weight of raw metal, each being worth 20 Lunars in lands where their legality is recognised. One ENC of pure gold provides the raw material for about 60 wheels.

blocks.name: Comfort Song
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: All those within range of the caster feel the magic of this Song of Power, which keeps all hearers from feeling pain, effectively anesthetizing the body while still permitting consciousness. It is often used during childbirth and after battles. Characters that would normally be incapacitated from the pain of injuries up to Serious Wounds are not, though damage is unhealed.

blocks.name: Command (Specific Species, Monster or Spirit)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Hundreds of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Command allows the caster to take over the actions and will of the specified creature, which fails to resist. If successful then whilst within range the creature will be in a special form of telepathic communication with the caster. The caster must form a mental image of the actions that he wishes the creature to perform. Commanding a creature requires a full round of concentration.

blocks.name: Command Worshippers
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Days), Ranged (Kilometres), Rank Initiate, Runes Any Elemental Rune, or [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: Casting this spell causes all initiates and priests of the cult to hastily finish immediate business, gather equipment and report to the caster as soon as possible. Command Worshipper must be cast at a shrine or temple to work, and during this time the caster must remain in the same spot or else the spell disperses. It is generally used only for emergencies or for joyous celebrations.

uncertain_readings: Rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised to rune names in several stat lines.
corrections_applied: Expanded broken line wraps.
Normalised spell headings and RuneQuest references.

blocks.heading: Gods of Dragon Pass
blocks.intro: The following list is a non-exclusive list of the main gods of Dragon Pass, some of which are detailed in the Cults chapter on page XXX.

entries.name: Asrelia
entries.text: Goddess of the Earth's Wealth, which she keeps in a treasure chamber far beneath the earth. Each winter she collects everything and counts it, and each spring returns what she wishes to return.

entries.name: Babeester Gor
entries.text: Avenging Goddess and Guardian, she wields a deadly axe and drinks ale made with the blood of her victims.

entries.name: Barntar
entries.text: The Plowman, the peaceful farmer-god, and backbone of the clan. He is the son of Ernalda and Orlanth.

entries.name: Chalana Arroy
entries.text: Goddess of Health and Healing, and on a larger scale of Compassion and Mercy. She is one of the Seven Lightbringers.

entries.name: Daka Fal
entries.text: The Judge of the Dead, who reviews the lives of mortals and sends each soul to its proper and just reward.

entries.name: Donandar
entries.text: The Bard of the Gods, whose instrument is the small harp.

entries.name: Eiritha
entries.text: Goddess of the Praxian herd beasts.

entries.name: Elmal
entries.text: God of the Sun. He was rescued by Orlanth and married into the Storm Tribe. He is a loyal thane, and guarded the homestead when the Lightbringers departed.

entries.name: Ernalda
entries.text: The primary goddess. As Goddess of the Earth Powers she is head of a pantheon of agricultural entities. As wife of Orlanth and mother of many others, she is the Family Goddess as well, with a pantheon of household spirits. She is also the independent leader of the goddesses, and Queen of the Earth Tribe in her own right.

entries.name: Esra
entries.text: Goddess of Grains, more specifically the Barley Mother.

entries.name: Esrola
entries.text: Goddess of the Physical Earth.

entries.name: Eurmal
entries.text: Trickster God, Fool, Lawbreaker, Outlaw, and Scapegoat. He is nonetheless one of the Seven Lightbringers.

entries.name: Flamal
entries.text: God of Vegetation.

entries.name: Foundchild
entries.text: The Hunter God of the Praxians.

entries.name: Gustbran the Bonesmith
entries.text: Redsmith of the Gods, this is the moral smith who makes the magical implements of the immortals.

entries.name: Heler
entries.text: God of Rain, originally of the Water Tribe but now a loyal ally of Orlanth.

entries.name: Hon-eel
entries.text: The Artess, the Dancer, and Second Inspiration of Moonson; this New Goddess is the goddess of maize, fertility, and rebirth for the Lunar Empire. She is also the ancestor-patron of the ruling dynasty in Tarsh.

entries.name: Humakt
entries.text: Son of Umath, brother of Orlanth. God of War and Death, and of all Endings. He even severed his family connections with the Storm Tribe to maintain his purity of purpose. He is chaste and/or sterile.

entries.name: Issaries
entries.text: God of Trade and Travel, and also of Communication in general. He is one of the Seven Lightbringers.

entries.name: Kero Fin
entries.text: Goddess of Dragon Pass, the ancient name of the land was Kerofinela. She is also the Mountain Goddess inhabiting the great peak in the center of the land. She is the mother of Orlanth and Yinkin.

entries.name: Kolat
entries.text: A Spirit, and member of the Orlanth Tribe. He is the First Spirit-master, who commands the Wind Spirits and aided Orlanth during the Gods War.

entries.name: Lhankor Mhy
entries.text: God of Knowledge and Writing. He is one of the Seven Lightbringers.

entries.name: Maran Gor
entries.text: Goddess of Earthquakes and Destruction, she is Ernalda's sister.

entries.name: Mastakos
entries.text: Charioteer of the Gods, personal driver to Orlanth, and in general, the God of Motion.

entries.name: Odayla
entries.text: God of Hunting in all its fashions: the chase from horseback, the stalk with bow and arrow, the stampede of herds over cliffs, the capture of great monsters in pits or with humble trap lines and deadfalls.

entries.name: Orlanth
entries.text: King of the Gods, and principle deity of the pantheon. He epitomizes all that a man can be in the Orlanthi society. He is the Chief of the Storm Tribe. He destroyed the world by loosing the power of Death, and he created it anew by leading the Seven Lightbringers to liberate life and light from Death.

entries.name: Pelora
entries.text: Goddess of Grain for the northern Orlanthi regions, more specifically the Wheat Mother.

entries.name: Red Goddess
entries.text: The Goddess of the Red Moon, Sister of Chaos, Lurker on the Veil, and Goddess of the Lunar Empire.

entries.name: Sartar
entries.text: Legendary character and Founder of the Kingdom of Sartar, who performed his great deeds from 1492 to 1520.

entries.name: Seven Mothers
entries.text: The original secret council that restored the Red Goddess to the world receives popular worship throughout the Lunar Empire as New Gods.

entries.name: Ty Kora Tek
entries.text: Goddess of the Dead, and the Land of the Dead. She is a gaunt and wasted deity whose worshippers prepare corpses and guard graveyards.

entries.name: Urox
entries.text: God of the Desert Storm, a potentially harmful wind which sometimes blows into Dragon Pass from the east. He is unruly and lawless, but has powers that are specifically useful to combat Chaos. He is usually called Storm Bull.

entries.name: Valind
entries.text: God of Winter, whose land is the wide realm to the north which is covered with ice forever. In winter the Valind Winds blow snow storms off the ice cap and south across the continent, so that they gather at Dragon Pass.

entries.name: Vinga
entries.text: Goddess of Woman Adventurers. Worshippers dye their hair red. She is worshipped as part of the Orlanth cult.

entries.name: Waha
entries.text: God of the Praxians. He shows people how to survive in Prax and the Wastelands.

entries.name: Yelmalio
entries.text: God of the Winter Sun, Preserver of the Light. When Yelm travelled to the Underworld, Yelmalio preserved the dim, cold light until he returned. He also fought against Orlanth in the Gods War.

entries.name: Yinkin
entries.text: God of Alynxes, the feline companions of the Orlanthi people. Yinkin is sometimes Orlanth's secret perception in a story, which leads him to success.

uncertain_readings: The white-on-black callout is degraded. Some rune glyphs and a few descriptive epithets are uncertain.
The word 'Artess' in the Hon-eel entry is visually uncertain.
The Eiritha entry appears partially run into the Elmal entry; only the clearly readable portion is retained.
corrections_applied: Inverted and visually inspected the rendered callout to improve readability.
Expanded broken line wraps and corrected obvious scan spacing.

blocks.name: Community
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: This spell guarantees easy communication of ideas, facilitating the sharing of new or troubled thoughts. It affects every individual within range who belongs to one of the same communities as the caster. All INT related skill rolls, providing the user knows that skill, are automatically successful when utilised to advance the common interests of that community. A "community" is assumed to be a group no larger than several hundred members, such as a family, clan, village, town district, cult, school, guild or even mercenary company.

blocks.name: Conceal/Concealment
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Conceal renders the recipient both invisible and inaudible for the duration of the spell, provided they perform no offensive or destructive action. If so, the concealment temporarily fades, revealing them to observers until they cease whatever action revealed them, at which point they fade back into invisibility at the end of the round. For the purposes of combat, the recipient must first disengage from their opponent before they can disappear again.

blocks.name: Consume
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Turns), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: If the target fails to resist, Consume inflicts 1d3 damage to a random Hit Location, ignoring all armour. On each subsequent turn of the caster, the target takes another 1d3 damage to the same place, continuing until the location reaches a Major Wound. At this point it is utterly destroyed, turned into grey slime, possibly killing the target if it was a vital location. If the spell ends prematurely before turning to slime, any damage done can be healed normally.

blocks.name: Consume Mind
blocks.stat_line: Duration (Instant), Ranged (Metres), Rank Priest, Resist (Willpower), Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell drains the entirety of the victim's memory, transferring it to the caster's mind and leaving the victim a mental vegetable. It can be cast only during the cult's holy day and takes a week to prepare for the ritual. Such diabolical efforts come at severe risk however, since whether the spell succeeds or not, the caster automatically takes 1d6+4 damage to their head. In addition they must also pass an unopposed Endurance check, else roll on the Consume Mind Backlash table.

blocks.title: Consume Mind Backlash Table

rows.effect: Caster permanently loses 1d3 INT
Caster permanently loses 1d3 POW
Caster suffers incoherent gibbering
Soul of victim immediately attacks caster in spirit combat
Caster permanently loses 1d10 Basic Magic spells
Caster permanently loses 1d4 Rune Magic spells
Caster permanently loses the Consume Mind spell
Caster suffers catatonic shock lasting 1d6 days
Caster suffers catatonic shock lasting 1d6 weeks
Caster's own memories lost and replaced by those of the victim. In effect the victim now inhabits the caster's body.

blocks.notes: The table is heavily degraded. Roll 3 is reconstructed from visible fragments and likely meaning.

blocks.name: Consume Mind
blocks.text: If the spell succeeds, the caster may learn any or all of the victim's magic and knowledge-based skills. Divine spells so obtained are reusable, provided they were also reusable to the victim. In such cases the caster can power stolen Rune Spells with Magic Points from their own Devotional Pool. Knowledge and Magic-related skills known by the victim at a level higher than the priest's own ability may also be gained. For each such skill already known, the caster may attempt a single improvement roll as if learning by experience, whereas new skills hitherto unknown simply start off at base value. None of skills or improvements gained in this manner can exceed the value of the victim's former skill level.

blocks.name: Conversion
blocks.text: This spell is no longer supported, as permanent Chaos gifts now always taint those who are not Illuminates.

blocks.name: Corruption
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Rounds), Ranged (Metres), Rank Priest, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Chaos-like glyph]
blocks.text: If not resisted, this spell transforms the victim into a gruesome chaos horror. At the start of each Round, roll 2d6 for each of the target's characteristics. If the 2d6 result is odd, subtract it from the characteristic. If the result is even, add it to the characteristic. The 2d6 roll is always subtracted from CHA. If any characteristic except CHA is reduced to 0 or less, the being becomes incapable of survival and dies immediately. At the conclusion of the spell, whatever values the characteristics reached become permanent, as are the

Consume Mind Backlash roll 3 is visually uncertain.

Expanded broken words and line wraps.

blocks.name: Corruption
blocks.text: physical deformities. Note: This spell takes over from the miracle described in the RuneQuest core rules, which itself has been renamed to 'Chastise'.

blocks.name: Counter Chaos
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Shields the recipient against the effects of any special ability a monster or being may get from its connections with chaos. Thus if a chaotic creature has a Chaos Feature increasing its natural Armour Points from 3 to 9, it is reduced back to 3 for attacks made by the protected character. A chaotic monster with Regeneration cannot regenerate from wounds made by the protected character, and so on. Note that this effect occurs no matter whether the Chaos Feature is advantageous or disadvantageous.

blocks.name: Couvade
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Special), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: A spell for midwifery, Couvade is cast upon a pregnant female and the father, or suitable proxy, of the unborn child, both of whom must consent to be included in this spell and be present at its casting. If cast within the first season of pregnancy, all the pains and sickness of pregnancy will affect him instead of her. The spell's duration lasts until the child is born. During delivery, the woman suffers only minimal pangs, but the husband is prostrate with pain; ensuring the safety of both mother and child. This spell is especially important for trolls, as they have much difficulty in childbearing, especially with the large great troll births and with trollkin. This spell does not, unfortunately, enable the mother to be safe from birthing trollkin, but they are born alive and healthy.

blocks.name: Crack
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast at a non-living, non-magical object, which can anything from a piece of armour or a weapon, up to a roof beam or statue. Fetishes containing spirits and objects with temporal spells cast upon them do not count as 'magical' in this circumstance, only items made from magical substances or those that are permanently enchanted, such as spell matrices. The targeted object immediately breaks into pieces, provided its SIZ is no larger than the Intensity of the magic. A Repair spell can fix the broken object.

blocks.name: Create Bonfire
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: Create Bonfire must be cast on an existing flame or fire, increasing its diameter to the value of the spell's Intensity in metres. Although the flames can spread and hence illuminate, the actual temperature of the flames does not increase. If no substance is available for the expanded fire to burn, it rapidly dwindles back to its former status. This spell has no effect on magical flames such as Fireblade or similar spells. It will however increase a salamander's size by one cubic metre per two full points of Create Bonfire, augmenting its Hit Points and protection, but not its damage or other characteristics.

blocks.name: Create Fissure
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Evade), Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Create Fissure opens up a massive crack in the ground, parting the soil to either undermine a structure such as a wall or creating a physical obstacle difficult to cross. The dimensions of the chasm depend upon the strength of the magic, having a length equal to Intensity in metres, a depth of half that, and a width equal to one third of the length. Thus an Intensity 6 Create Fissure spell would form a crevasse 6m long, 3m deep and 2m wide. If the crack is cast to undermine a wall or similar construction, the structure loses 1d10 Hit Points per point of Intensity, ignoring structural armour (RuneQuest page 12). If reduced to 0 Hit Points, it collapses where it has been undermined. If the chasm is formed directly underneath a target who fails to Evade, they fall into it, taking appropriate falling damage (RuneQuest page 118).

blocks.name: Create Flippers
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This turns the recipient's arms and legs into armoured flippers incapable of performing any manipulation skill. Any Swim skill checks are made one difficulty grade easier. His swimming Movement Rate becomes 6 and his speed on land is reduced to 1. The recipient's skin on his limbs becomes leathery, granting a number of natural Armour Points equal to half the spell's Intensity.

uncertain_readings: Create Fissure page reference appears to read RuneQuest page 12, but may be page 122 or another page due scan loss.
Several Rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.
corrections_applied: Merged the Corruption continuation from the previous page.
Expanded hyphenated and broken line wraps.

blocks.name: Create Foe-Curser
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Weeks), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a totem involving the skull of some person or creature, often set into an alcove, crevasse or atop a post. After it is created, the foe-curser will trigger its magic on trespassers entering the area who register as hostile to the caster, community or cult the spell was cast to protect. The type of curse depends on the skull being used, and lasts until dispelled or the next Sacred Time.

curse_types.effect: Prevents the victim from casting any type of spell which costs more than a single Magic Point.
The victim becomes night blind; any partial darkness becomes pitch black to them.
Movement Rate of the victim is halved.
Howls warningly at a pitch, which may or may not be audible to other races as desired by the caster.
Victims become Demoralised as per the Folk Magic spell.
The victim automatically fails any Fatigue roll.

blocks.name: Create Ghost
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Special), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death-like glyph, other glyphs visible]
blocks.text: The Create Ghost ritual must be performed over the body of a recently slain victim and takes several hours to complete. The ritual tears the soul of the person from the path that the soul naturally follows after death, and causes it to appear at the location of its old body and attack the caster in spirit combat. If the soul's Magic Points are brought to zero, it is turned into a haunt (RuneQuest page 406), permanently bound at that place. If on the other hand the soul wins, it either possesses the caster's body to wreak havoc, or possibly forces them to commit suicide, depending on the whim of soul before it departs once more for its afterlife.

blocks.name: Create Gorp
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Special), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Chaos-like glyph]
blocks.text: Another ritual spell which is performed over the earth itself, it transforms a portion of the ground into a live gorp with a SIZ equal to three times the spell's Intensity. It does not however, provide any control over the chaos monstrosity.

blocks.name: Create Great Market
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Rank Devotee, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: This spell enables members of the same cult to combine and overlap their Create Market spells so that its collective Area and Duration are based upon the combined Intensity of the participants. A modestly sized Create Great Market cast with seven Create Market spells of Intensity 6, 6, 7, 7, 8 and 9 would grant it an Area of 49m radius and a Duration of five days.

blocks.name: Create Head
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Special), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death-like glyph, Darkness-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast on the cult's Holy Day or High Holy Day. In this ritual, a living victim is decapitated with a silver garrotte and their soul trapped within the severed, living head. The head is placed in a mental connection with the caster, and its magic points become available for casting spells. All Folk, Rune, Spirit and Sorcery magic the victim knew are usable by the caster, who must use victim's own skills to cast and manipulate them. Any Rune Spells known by a head can be used until the head's Devotional Pool is drained, at which point the caster will lose access to its Rune Magic. In addition, heads whose souls have not yet descended into gibbering madness may be questioned by the caster, who must succeed in an unopposed Influence roll to gain a truthful answer. The duration of Create Head continues until the caster drops the spell, the head suffers a Major Wound or it is drained to zero Magic Points. Heads cannot recover Hit Points naturally, but must be healed using magic. Neither can a head regain Magic Points, unless the caster ritually recharges it with their own points at a cult shrine or temple. Depending on the personality and strength of will of the victim, each month the soul remains trapped in its own head it must succeed in an unopposed Willpower roll, else go insane; at which point the caster loses access to the head's magic and its knowledge. When the spell ends for whatever reason, the head decays into a stinking mass of corruption, and trapped souls which still possess Magic Points become Mad Head Ghosts, tied to the putrid remnants. Treat as Haunts (RuneQuest page 406).

blocks.name: Create Market
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Special), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: Create Market creates an invisible protected zone identical to Warding in most respects. The priest must use staffs one or more metres in height, carved with cult iconography to define

uncertain_readings: Create Head includes dense degraded text; the transcription follows the visible page closely.
Rune glyphs are visible but not always safely normalised.
corrections_applied: Converted the Create Foe-Curser bullet list into structured JSON.
Expanded line-wrap and spacing artifacts.

blocks.name: Create Market
blocks.text: the boundaries of the zone; this area may be of any shape, provided it lies within the limits of the spell. Unlike Warding it does not trigger on just anyone crossing the boundary, but only on those who enter the area with hostile intent, whether violence or theft.

blocks.name: Create Neutral Ground
blocks.text: As per Create Market.

blocks.name: Create Shadow
blocks.text: See Summon Shadow (page 116).

blocks.name: Create Skeleton
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Special), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [three visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Using the bones of a long dead or deliberately excarnated body, the caster animates the skeleton at the victim's old level of competence, with an upper limit of the caster's Devotion skill percentage. It works in a similar manner to the Raise Undead miracle (RuneQuest page 274), except that it only quickens a single skeleton. In return the skeleton endures indefinitely, until the caster eventually allows the Rune Spell to lapse. The raised skeleton is calculated as per the main rules (RuneQuest page 384), but gains the usual bonus to its STR and CON equal to the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Create Warrior of Wood
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Causes a tree, which has previously been ritually dedicated on the cult's High Holy Day, to awaken and serve the commands of the caster, so that it becomes capable of motion and combat. Use the Awaken miracle (RuneQuest page 264), treating its Armour Points as 6.

blocks.name: Create Whirvlish
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This ritual creates a Whirvlish from the soul of a person slain by the enchanter. The ritual must be performed under the desert sky and takes an entire night to finish. The person whose soul is to be transformed into the accursed spirit is murdered at the completion of the ceremony. See Whirvlish page 145.

blocks.name: Create Wildfire
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast on an existing fire, whether merely a candle flame or a bonfire, increasing its temperature. The Fire Intensity (RuneQuest page 120) is raised by 1 for each two full points of spell Intensity. Although heat is increased, the size of the original fire is not. However, fires augmented beyond Intensity 5 explode from the rapid thermal release, scattering flaming fragments of whatever was burning to a distance equal to the spell's Range; this effect can spread the fire further if they land on readily combustible material. At the conclusion of the spell the original fire burns itself out, having consumed all its fuel and leaving nothing behind but ashes.

blocks.name: Create Zombie
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Special), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [three visible glyphs]
blocks.text: As per the Create Skeleton spell, except that it raises a zombie from a corpse instead.

blocks.name: Cremate Dead
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Death-like glyph, Fire/Sun-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to fully destroy the bodily remains of one or more cult members after death, guaranteeing that the soul will not return to haunt the family as a ghost or worse. It also burns all goods sent along with a corpse, allowing the deceased to carry some weapons and other supplies into the land of the dead. Cremate Dead can affect as many targets as the Intensity of the spell. It can also be used on the animated skeleton, zombie, mummified, or even vampire forms of former cult members, though the target may attempt to resist the magic in this case. Failure causes the undead target to spontaneously burst into flame and collapse as they are consumed.

blocks.name: Crush
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Rounds), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: Crush infuses the recipient's blunt weapon strikes with overwhelming force. For each five full points of Intensity, the size of the opponent's weapon is reduced one step when parrying. Thus an Intensity 6 Crush spell would decrease the size of a Hoplite shield or Greatsword from Huge to Large, whereas an Intensity 10 Crush would reduce the

Rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.
corrections_applied: Merged Create Market from previous page.
Expanded line-wrap and scan-spacing artifacts.

blocks.name: Crush
blocks.text: same parrying weapons to merely Medium, making this spell exceptionally dangerous.

blocks.name: Cure All Disease, Cure Brain Fever, Cure Chills, Cure Shakes, Cure Soul Waste, Cure Wasting
blocks.text: These spells have been amalgamated and replaced with Cure Disease (see below).

blocks.name: Cure Chaos Wound
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: Cure Chaos Wound is used to remove an affliction received from a chaotic creature or spell, for example reversing permanent changes caused by spells such as Chaos Feature or Corruption. It does not heal any damage per se, but can be combined with other healing spells to cure chaos-induced festering wounds, diseases or in the case of Broo, impregnations.

blocks.name: Cure Disease
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: This spell cures any disease currently afflicting the recipient, removing any conditions which are not considered permanent, such as Hit Point reduction due to Maiming or Characteristic loss. Note however that Cure Disease has no effect on disease bearing spirits which might be possessing the recipient.

blocks.name: Cure Iron Burn
blocks.text: This spell has been renamed to Resist Iron Burn (page 107).

blocks.name: Cure Poison
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is the same as Cure Disease except that it affects victims of poisoning.

blocks.name: Curse of Thed
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Months), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Chaos-like glyph]
blocks.text: As per Reverse Chaos (page 107), save that the spell may only be used against creatures of Chaos or those tainted by Chaos in some way. Provided the spell is maintained, the victim suffers the enduring effects of the curse.

blocks.name: Dark Fear
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Darkness glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: This spell operates much like Bump in the Night (page 80), but Dark Fear can be used in daylight to create a small area of demoralising shadow around the caster.

blocks.name: Darklight
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Special), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: Darklight creates an invisible illumination which can only be seen by cult members. It must be cast on any item which is capable of burning and giving off non-magical light, such as torches, lanterns, braziers, campfires and so on. Non-cultists can see the dim violet flames, but the fire illuminates nothing to them. The spell ends when the fuel is naturally expended.

blocks.name: Darksee
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Fire/Sun-like glyph]
blocks.text: Allows the recipient to see an environment alien to their senses. Diurnal creatures can see in the dark as if it were full daylight, and vice versa for nocturnal creatures. Darksee also cancels the effects of sunlight on trollkin and cave trolls, as well as negating the effects of the Blinding spell as well.

blocks.name: Dark Walk
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to be totally invisible and soundless in darkness and shadow. The blend in is total, but launching an attack reveals the caster until the end of the Round. For the purposes of close combat, the recipient must first disengage from their opponent before they can disappear again.

blocks.name: Decrease Temperature
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: Decrease Temperature lowers the temperature by 1d30 Centigrade per point of Intensity, with the possible result of inducing exposure to those within the area (RuneQuest page 129). If the temperature drops below freezing point, any precipitation will turn to snow and standing water will slowly freeze over.

blocks.name: Decrease Wind
blocks.stat_line: Area (Hundreds of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]

uncertain_readings: Several Rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.
corrections_applied: Merged Crush continuation from previous page.
Normalised the Cure spell group into a structured group entry.

blocks.name: Decrease Wind
blocks.text: This spell decreases the average wind STR by 1d10 per point of Intensity for the duration of the spell (RuneQuest page 130). This spell has no effect upon temperature or wind direction.

blocks.name: Defend Against Chaos
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: Defend Against Chaos helps the caster to avoid being overwhelmed by chaotic creatures or enemies possessing an affinity to the Chaos Rune. Any Evade roll used to outmanoeuvre chaos foes in combat has its Level of Success boosted by one step. Thus a Fumble upgrades to a Fail, a Fail to a Success and a Success to a Critical, whilst rolled Critical Successes cannot be increased any higher. Under its influence, the caster is usually able to stay out of reach whilst whittling down the opposition one by one.

blocks.name: Defend Against Law
blocks.text: As per Defend Against Chaos, but works on foes without a Chaos Aura, Chaos Feature or a link to the Chaos Rune.

blocks.name: Detect Truth
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to tell whether anyone within the radius of the spell is lying. If lies are spoken, the speaker emits a dark, smoky glow visible to members of the cult.

blocks.name: Detection Blank
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Detection Blank is cast to screen any object, spell or person from detection by Sense, Detect, or Find magic of equal or lower Intensity. Unlike other anti-magic protections like Countermagic and similar spells, it does not let the person doing the detection know that his spell has been blocked.

blocks.name: Devour (Race)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to ritually consume a still-living person according to the specific race or species defined. Common versions include Devour Elf, Devour Human and Devour Troll. Over the course of the gruesome feast, the caster absorbs Magic Points from the suffering victim. The amount gained cannot exceed either the Intensity of the spell or the victim's CON, whichever is lower. These Magic Points replenish the caster's own, and any surplus can temporarily exceed the caster's maximum, but dissipate once the spell ends.

blocks.name: Devour (Substance)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: The caster may eat any amount of the indicated substance without harm. Thus someone who casts Devour Chaos could eat a gorp, and would take no damage from its acidic nature nor gain any chaotic taint; whereas Devour Plant would protect against thorns and plant-based poisons; Devour Fire would allow the quaffing of flaming oil without burns; and so on. In addition to any nutritional benefits, the spell ensures that all of the ingested substance is fully digested and out of the caster's system by the time it expires. Thus it is an invaluable way of dealing with regenerating monsters or substances which are harmful to the caster such as iron.

blocks.name: Devour Book
blocks.text: See Ingest Scroll page 99.

blocks.name: Devour Spirit
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Rounds), Rank Initiate, Runes [two visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Devour Spirit allows the caster to consume the Magic Points of spirits engaged in Spirit Combat. Any damage roll inflicted upon the spirit is absorbed by the caster instead. Thus a caster currently at 7 MP facing a Haunt at 11 MP, who inflicted a 3 points of damage on the spirit, would steal those Magic Points, restoring them to 10 MP whilst the spirit is decreased to 8 MP. A spirit reduced to zero Magic Points under the effects of this spell is utterly destroyed.

blocks.name: Diamond Edge
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: When cast on stone cutting tools such as pickaxes or mallets, this spell increases the damage inflicted by an amount equal to the Intensity of the spell, but only when used against creatures or objects made of earth and stone. Thus an Intensity 8 Diamond Edge cast on a pickaxe (1d6) would allow the weapon to inflict 1d6+8 damage against gnomes, gargoyles or a stone fortification.

blocks.name: Discorporation
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Kilometres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyph]

uncertain_readings: Discorporation begins at the bottom of the page and continues on the next page.
Rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.

Expanded broken line wraps and normalised punctuation.

blocks.name: Discorporation
blocks.text: The caster temporarily detaches the spirit of the touched recipient, allowing it to become a spirit and travel from their body up to the spell's range. Awareness of the recipient resides in their spirit form, so that their body remains comatose until the spell ends. Unwilling recipients may resist. A discorporated character is treated in all respects as an ordinary disembodied spirit, thus they are not only visible but also recognisable. The discorporated recipient may travel through physical objects without harm and can swiftly travel to known locations within range. In addition they are still able to cast magic, although their spirit is also vulnerable to magical attacks. Any fumble rolled during their discorporate sojourn immediately withdraws them back to their body. If their body is killed whilst still under the effects of this spell, the dead recipient may attempt one task before being drawn into whatever afterlife awaits them.

blocks.name: Dismiss Elemental
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Runes [Air glyph, Darkness glyph, Earth glyph, Fire/Sun glyph, Moon glyph, Water glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Dismiss Elemental miracle (RuneQuest page 268).

blocks.name: Dismiss Magic
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Dismiss Magic miracle (RuneQuest page 268).

blocks.name: Divination
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes Any
blocks.text: Divination allows the caster to communicate with their deity, but must be cast within a temple or shrine to that god in a ritual lasting one hour per two points of Intensity. At the conclusion of the ritual, the caster may ask one simple question per hour invested in the ceremony. In return he or she either will receive an answer of up to seven words or a brief vision or dream. However, the Game Master should privately roll against the caster's Devotion skill for each question. A failure implies that the caster unsuccessfully interpreted the answer or dream properly, resulting in a misleading or puzzling answer. Note: Deities are incapable of revealing what they do not know. The only areas over which they have any familiarity are their personal experiences during the God Time; the last known location and activities of their initiates and devotees, as reported during that worshipper's most recent prayers; and general information concerning events within the god's sphere of control of their Runes.

blocks.name: Divination Block
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Divination Block must be cast on either a single person or object. During the period of the spell, the presence of the recipient or any activities it is involved with, are obscured from magical observation. This obscurement affects history itself, so that Divination or any other knowledge spells cast after the fact cannot recall any details of what occurred during the period of the Divination Block. However, the existence of a Divination Block having been used is not concealed.

blocks.name: Divine Intervention
blocks.text: Divine Intervention is no longer used in RuneQuest 6th Edition. Devotees instead receive the ability to channel their god directly via Feats.

blocks.name: Draw Beast
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Hundreds of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Beast glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: When this spell is cast upon one or more beasts possessing INS instead of INT, up to a number equal to the spell's Intensity. Those creatures which fail to resist move toward the caster at walking speed.

blocks.name: Draw Human
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Hundreds of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Man-like glyph, three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: As per the Draw Beast spell, except that it attracts INT-based races descended from the Man Rune.

blocks.name: Drown
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Rounds), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Water glyph]
blocks.text: Drown is only effective on air breathing targets, filling the target's lungs with water if they fail to resist. At the end of that Round and every Round thereafter, the victim must make an Endurance roll as per the Asphyxiation rules (RuneQuest page 108). Breathe Water or similar spells renders the target immune to Drown, but does not heal any Fatigue damage already taken.

blocks.name: Earthmight
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Earth glyph]
blocks.text: The recipient of Earthmight will gain 1d6 STR and 1d6 SIZ per two full points of Intensity, which may exceed its species maximums. It takes a full round to grow to full stature.

Draw Beast appears grammatically incomplete in the source; transcription preserves the visible wording.

blocks.name: Earthmight
blocks.text: The magic is only effective as long as the recipient remains in physical contact with unworked earth or stone. If this contact is ever lost, the spell ends.

blocks.name: Earthpower
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Special), Rank Devotee, Runes [Earth glyph]
blocks.text: Once cast this spell endures until it is activated. It is triggered when the caster's Magic Points drop to zero, providing they are in contact with the earth; whereupon they recover a number of Magic Points equal to a dice roll of a size commensurate to the Intensity of the spell, and recharge their Devotional Pool by a dice roll of half that size (see the Earthpower Effect table).

blocks.title: Earthpower Effect Table
blocks.notes: The table header is partly lost in the scan; column names are reconstructed from the spell text.

blocks.name: Earthtouch
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Ranged (Tens of Metres), Runes [Earth glyph]
blocks.text: Earthtouch permits the caster to learn the exact STR, CON, SIZ, DEX, INT, POW and CHA of any entity within range, providing that both the target being observed and the caster are touching the ground. It also informs the caster of the target's current Hit Points, Magic Points and Fatigue level. Only a single entity can be investigated at a time and it requires a Combat Action to change to a new target.

blocks.name: Earthwarm
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph, Earth glyph]
blocks.text: Causes an area of soil or rock to increase in temperature, by a number of °C equal to twice the Intensity of the spell. Earthwarm is normally used to protect fields and orchards against frosts, but can also be used to protect travellers in frigid climes when no other heat is available or even melt snow and ice to unblock mountain passes.

blocks.name: Ecstatic Communion
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Hours), Rank Devotee, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Ecstatic Communion must be cast on the cult Holy Day or High Holy Day. It is normally performed as part of a specific ritual embracing an aspect of that deity within the cult temple or shrine. All participants within the area experience sensual bliss unless they try to resist. Otherwise everyone involved, including the caster, are overwhelmed by the sensations of whatever ritual activity they are performing. Those under the effects of Ecstatic Communion feel no pain or discomfort, nor may they react to physical harm against them, but must continue the ritual to its climax. Only the caster may attempt to break free of the spell, requiring them to succeed in an unopposed roll against their Willpower. The pleasures granted by Ecstatic Communion are so addictive that anyone who succumbs to its power, whether willingly or not, will thereafter cooperate fully with the cult to experience the bliss again.

blocks.name: Enchant (Metal or Object)
blocks.text: All enchantments are now cult specific Feats which require a Heroquest to the other world to perform.

blocks.name: Enhance Gustbran
blocks.text: See Create Wildfire (page 88).

blocks.name: Enhance Mahome
blocks.text: See Create Bonfire (page 86).

blocks.name: Erotocomatose Lucidity
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: The spell affects both the caster and one intended recipient, who does not usually try to resist. Both the recipient and caster experience the ecstatic Oneness felt during the moment of sexual climax. The experience lasts for the entire duration of the spell. The experience is so strong that after it expires, both participants' Fatigue is increased by one level per two points of Intensity.

blocks.name: Exchange Spells
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]

Exchange Spells begins at the bottom of the page and continues on the next page.

blocks.name: Exchange Spells
blocks.text: This allows the user to trade knowledge of one Folk Magic spell. It cannot be used with Spirit Magic, Rune Spells or Sorcery. The user must set up a Mindlink connection between himself and his customer. The participants agree upon the spells they will attempt to trade, one spell each. If the Exchange Spells succeeds, each participant loses knowledge of the spell they chose and gains knowledge of the spell their partner chose. A fumble indicates that both participants lose knowledge of their own spell, and do not gain their partner's spell.

blocks.name: Excommunication
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Devotee, Runes Any
blocks.text: Use the default Excommunication miracle (RuneQuest page 270). The spell requires a preparatory ceremony of at least an hour at a cult temple or shrine before it may be cast. The target of the Excommunication need not be physically present, since the caster is requesting the god to perform the act of severance. Neither does the target gain a chance to resist, since the deity will not comply with the request unless the victim is truly guilty of the crimes they are accused of. In addition to losing their Devotional Pool, the victim will be pursued by the cult's Spirits of Retribution.

blocks.name: Extension
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Runes Any
blocks.text: Other than the reduced cost, use the default Extension miracle (RuneQuest page 270).

blocks.name: Face Chaos
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: When cast upon the recipient, that person stands their ground and fights any chaotic foe they face, even if ordinarily they would have run. If desired the spell may be resisted, but might lead to future accusations of cowardliness. This spell does not render the recipient immune to the effects of such spells as Demoralize or Panic, though they still will not flee.

blocks.name: Face Law
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: As per Face Chaos, but works against enemies without a Chaos Aura, Chaos Feature or a link to the Chaos Rune.

blocks.name: False Form
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Weeks), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell gives the recipient the semblance of a normal human to all senses, including non-human senses such as Darksense. This spell also hides any cult mark the recipient may possess. It is used by members of the cult to pass as normal humans. The spell is usually recast each seasonal cult Holy Day.

blocks.name: Fang of Wachaza
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Rounds), Rank Devotee, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Death-like glyph, Water glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a spear, javelin, dart or trident. The weapon's point(s) take on the appearance of old ivory while under the influence of this spell. Anyone struck and damaged by the weapon must resist, or they instantly die. Otherwise the victim simply takes the damage rolled.

blocks.name: Fangs
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Death-like glyph]
blocks.text: Fangs causes the growth of huge red teeth from the mouth of the recipient. Attacking with these fangs utilises the Unarmed skill of the user, and does the base damage of a bite attack (see Natural Weapon Damage table RuneQuest page 324), plus any damage modifier. Furthermore, the fangs secrete an acid, the strength of which depends on the Intensity of the spell: 1-5 Weak acid; 6-10 Strong acid; 11+ Concentrated acid.

blocks.name: Fear
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Air glyph, Darkness glyph, Water glyph]
blocks.text: As per the default Fear miracle (RuneQuest page 270), save that the result of the opposed roll is nuanced. If the target is overcome, refer to the Fear table to judge the results of their Willpower roll:

blocks.title: Fear Table

rows.effect: Victim acts in all ways as if affected by the Demoralize spell.
Victim flees in terror.
Victim collapses unconscious.
Victim dies of fear.

blocks.notes: The table is partially degraded but the four result rows are legible.

blocks.name: Featherscales
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Causes feathery clouds of loose, sticky scales to coat the caster's body, increasing their natural Armour Points by half the value of the spell's Intensity. Additionally, when the character is hit by any weapon, that weapon becomes gummed up and covered with scales, making it blunt and padded. Such weapons henceforth do only half damage till cleaned off. Featherscales does not work if the caster is wearing armour.

blocks.name: Fertilize
blocks.stat_line: Area (Special), Cost 1, Duration (Months), Rank Devotee, Runes [Earth glyph, Fertility-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast as a daylong ritual. Whilst maintained by the caster it restores to the soil the necessary organic and mineral vitality to support plant life, whilst neutralising salts and other poisons. It is often used in regions which have been over-farmed, or are unnaturally desolate such as the Wastelands. Each point of Intensity fertilises an area of one hectare enough to feed a large family.

blocks.name: Fight Disease
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: When cast upon a sick individual, this spell helps to fight off the effects of a disease, mundane or magical, or Disease Spirit they are suffering from. Whilst the spell is in effect, all attempts to resist the disease or possession are doubled, as if the difficulty modifier of the roll was Very Easy.

blocks.name: Find (Specific Thing)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Hundreds of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes Any
blocks.text: This spell informs the caster of the presence of the specific substance, object or creature sought — illuminating it with a slight glow that can be seen only by the caster. This spell can detect all incidences within range, unless they are shielded behind a metre of solid material. The caster remains aware of the sought after presence for the duration of the spell. Each cult Find spell is specific to a single Rune-related thing such as fresh water, horses, enchanted objects and the like.

blocks.name: Firedwell
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph]
blocks.text: Allows the recipient to live midst volcanic conditions, so that no magical fire or heat will affect them. Additionally the spell permits them to see clearly through smoke or fumes, and breathe any noxious mephitic vapour. On the other hand, the recipient will feel chilled at normal room temperature and be susceptible to cold, taking double damage from magic of the Darkness Rune.

blocks.name: Firespear
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Devotee, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph]
blocks.text: This is cast upon a short spear, causing its point to burst into flame. The normal damage done by the weapon is replaced by 1d6 damage per two points of Intensity. The wielder of the weapon still adds his damage bonus when he strikes, and once the spell concludes the weapon emerges unharmed by the magical fire.

blocks.name: Fireshield
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Water glyph]
blocks.text: Fireshield reduces the damage of all fire-based attacks by half the Intensity of the spell. Thus an initiate casting the spell at an Intensity 7 would reduce the damage taken from a bonfire by 4 points, and be immune to the flames of a Firebladed weapon (+1d3 damage) entirely.

blocks.name: Fix Intelligence
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Rank Devotee, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell only affects members of the Elder Races linked with the Man Rune. If the target fails to resist the spell, his INT is permanently transformed into INS. The victim is now ruled by instinct, rather than reason. He remembers his former life and knows his friends and enemies. Essentially, he becomes a very cunning animal in humanoid form. The victim becomes totally incapable of using any knowledge or language skills, or using any magic except magic items which automatically function. He still has full use of his weapons, and those Standard skills whose default value is not based on INT. If this spell takes effect on a shaman, the
