blocks.name: Fix Intelligence
blocks.text: shaman's fetch takes over control of the body, but remains limited to those skills and spells it itself knows.

blocks.name: Flaming Bow
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Devotee, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph]
blocks.text: As per the Firespear spell, save that each arrow shot from the bow is consumed by flames as it travels towards a foe, inflicting the burning damage but preventing the impale special effect.

blocks.name: Flay
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast on a whip, flail or knout. Each two points of Intensity increases the base damage dice of the weapon by one step. For instance, a barbed whip does 1d3 base damage. Under the effects of an Intensity 5 Flay spell however, its damage would be incremented by 3 steps, to 1d8 damage instead. Note that bonuses to the base dice remain unaffected. Thus if the whip in the previous example had the effective enhancement it would start with a damage of 1d3+1, and be boosted to 1d8+1.

blocks.title: Base Weapon Dice Progression Table
blocks.notes: The table is degraded; the visible single-column progression is reconstructed from the legible entries.

blocks.name: Flight
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Resist (Special), Runes [Air glyph, Moon glyph]
blocks.text: The caster may transport one person, or a thing, up to SIZ 20 through the air for the duration of the spell. An unwilling target may attempt to Evade the grasp of the magic in an opposed roll, and if successful the spell fails. The maximum Movement Rate of the recipient is equal to the Intensity of the spell, though running or sprinting increases this as normal (RuneQuest page 105). However the ambient wind does affect the recipient, blowing them along at a Movement Rate equal to the STR of the breeze, or slowing them by that much if they are heading into the wind. The source of the spell limits how and where the recipient may fly. The Air Rune allows freedom to travel anywhere, but at the expense of never stopping, else they will waft gently to the ground. The Moon Rune only allows recipients to travel to and from the moon in question, but permits them to hang motionless in the sky if desired.

blocks.name: Float
blocks.text: See Buoyancy (page 80).

blocks.name: Flowers
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Earth glyph, Fertility-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the user to create flowers at will until the magic ends. During concentration, the ground and existing plants around the caster erupt with pretty flowers and sprays of unfurling leaves. If the site is suitable for a small plant's continued growth, it takes root and sprouts. If the site is not suitable, such as the chinks in stone walls or a tiled floor, the flowers wither when the spell concludes.

blocks.name: Forlorn Hope
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Hours), Rank Devotee, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: All cult members within range who fail to resist are compelled to fight fanatically for the cult. Any sense of fear or self preservation is lost, replaced with a desire to spend their lives dearly for their god. In order to maintain this devotion however, the caster must remain with the affected group of lay members and initiates, exposing themselves to the same danger. Cult members wearing enchanted slave collars or shackles (page XX) automatically fail to resist this spell.

blocks.name: Form Ice
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [Water glyph, Cold/Darkness-like glyph]
blocks.text: Form Ice transforms all open liquids into solid ice within its area of effect, down to a thickness equal to twice the Intensity in centimetres. The ice so formed can bear creatures with

Form Ice continues on the next page.
Several Rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.

Expanded broken line-wrap artifacts.

blocks.name: Form Ice
blocks.text: a SIZ up to three times the Intensity. The mobility of the fluid limits whether it can be frozen, with initiates only able to freeze the surface of static water such as pools or lakes, whilst devotees can freeze flowing rivers or the surface of the restless sea. Unless the ambient temperature is below 0°C, the ice will melt naturally of its own accord.

blocks.name: Free Ghost
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Man-like glyph, Spirit-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell can only be cast upon the Haunt or Wraith of a deceased mortal. If it fails to resist, the ghost is unbound from its haunting spot and returns to the Land of the Dead.

blocks.name: Fumble
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Rounds), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell is usually targeted against an opponent in combat. If the target fails to resist, then their next skill roll, provided it takes place within the spell's duration, will automatically become a fumble. The results of the fumble depend entirely on the skill being used.

blocks.name: Gift Power
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Days), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: Gift Power temporarily links the caster with a spirit, allowing him to transfer part of his Devotional Pool to augment the spirit's POW. The increase not only adds to the spirit's available Magic Points, but may also boost its Spirit Intensity. The maximum number of points the caster can transfer from his pool to the spirit is equal to the Intensity of the spell. Obviously the caster cannot access the gifted points of his pool until the spell ends, and even then he must recharge them first.

blocks.name: Gift Spell
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Days), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to transfer the knowledge of any one Folk Magic spell to another person, or a spirit with the Spellcasting ability. The caster loses knowledge of the spell whilst it is transferred, but reclaims it once the duration of the spell ends, or he ceases to maintain it.

blocks.name: Glow Spot
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [Moon glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes a beam of crimson light to descend from the Red Moon, illuminating the area around the recipient. This acts as a Glowspot, allowing all Lunar magic cast within its radius to function as it does on the day of the full moon. For this effect to work on ranged spells, both the caster and target must be within the radius of this or another Glowspot.

blocks.name: Gnome to Gargoyle
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Earth glyph, Stasis-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a gnome. It turns the earth elemental into a humanoid monster. The Characteristics and Attributes of the gargoyle remain those of the original gnome; however, it loses its ability to magically manipulate earth and rock. In exchange, the gargoyle gains Armour Points equal to the Intensity of the spell, plus the Hit Locations of its namesake (page 168). The damage each location can take is calculated by applying the gnome's total Hit Points on the Hit Points per Location table (RuneQuest page 14), noting that the gargoyle's arms have the same HP as its legs, whereas its wings have 1 HP less. When the spell expires, the monster turns back into earth.

blocks.name: Great Parry
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Earth glyph, Stasis-like glyph]
blocks.text: Great Parry must be cast upon a shield. It grants the shield an infinitely large Size in terms of blocking damage from attacks, although Knockback still affects the holder of the shield. So the wielder of the shield, providing they succeed in parrying, could use a buckler to stop all damage from the nose horn of a charging rhino, but would still be knocked flying.

blocks.name: Group Defense
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: Creates a shadowy sphere centred on the caster, with a radius up to half the Intensity of the spell. All those contained within are protected within its veil-like barrier. Any creature attempting to pass this barrier is automatically struck in a random Hit Location, ignoring armour, with damage equal to the Intensity of the spell. If two or more casters link

Group Defense continues on the next page.

Corrected line-wrap and spacing artifacts.

blocks.name: Group Defense
blocks.text: their Group Defense spells, the amount of damage inflicted by the conjoined barrier becomes the sum of the Intensities. Whilst the spell is active, the caster cannot perform any aggressive act.

blocks.name: Group Laughter
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell ensures that everyone within the area sees the humour of every joke told, and old jokes that everyone knows seem funny again. Indeed those under its effects are even capable of enjoying practical jokes played on themselves. Due to its harmless nature, the spell's effects cannot be resisted.

blocks.name: Guided Teleportation
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Special), Rank Devotee, Runes [Movement glyph]
blocks.text: Guided Teleportation returns the caster to a specific spot sacred to the god who provides the magic, usually a shrine or temple. This spell has no range limitation, but its destinations are obviously limited.

blocks.name: Hallucinate
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Illusion-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use Illusion (RuneQuest page 271), save that the spell only affects the caster and those in Mindlink with him. The hallucinations are completely undetectable to anyone else.

blocks.name: Hate Sky
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes all non-magical flames within the area of an Intensity equal or less than half the Intensity of the spell to be extinguished. Thus an initiate who cast this spell at an Intensity of 5 would instantly smother all flames up to and including campfires (Intensity 3 fires). Hate Sky also affects magical fires with lesser Magnitude, so a sufficiently potent casting would extinguish a Firespear or Elemental Summoning used to summon a salamander.

blocks.name: Heal Area
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Devotee, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: This spell heals any wound in a single hit location, including attaching a severed limb, provided it is cast within a number of minutes not exceeding the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Heal Body
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Rank Devotee, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: Use Heal Body (RuneQuest page 271).

blocks.name: Heal Constitution
blocks.text: See Cure Poison (page 89).

blocks.name: Heal Wound
blocks.text: See Heal Area (above).

blocks.name: Healing Trance
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: The recipient of this spell will fall into a deep trance, during which his bodily processes will be sped up by a multiple equal to the Intensity of the spell. Casting the spell with an Intensity of 8 on an injured warrior would speed up his natural Healing Rate by a factor of 8:1, resulting in the restoration of Hit Points in one eighth the time: so Minor Wounds would recover every 3 hours, Serious Wounds every 21 hours and Major Wounds every four days. Both the caster and the recipient remain in an unwakeable trance state until all healing is done. They must be fed and cared for as if they were catatonics during this period.

blocks.name: Heatblast
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Hundreds of Metres), Rank Devotee, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Fire/Sun glyph]
blocks.text: Heatblast can only be used on earth which is at a minimum of 40 degrees C, either naturally via geothermal activity, or through the Divine spell Earthwarm. It concentrates this ambient heat, then releases it in a burst, affecting everyone in the area who fails to resist. The blast inflicts 1d6 damage per 2 points of Intensity to every Hit Location, although natural and worn armour will help reduce the burns. Casting this spell drains the surrounding ground of warmth, preventing further castings of Heatblast until several days have passed to recuperate its thermal capacity.

blocks.name: Heat Metal
blocks.text: As per Create Wildfire (page 88), save that it can only be used on a forge fire and does not explode.

blocks.name: Hide Fire
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell is cast upon a container of any type or size, for instance a man's hand, a burlap sack or a pail. It permits the

blocks.name: Hide Fire
blocks.text: container to hold an appropriately sized fire without being burnt or putting the fire out. Thus the caster could pick a candle flame off its wick and hold in his palm for the duration of the fire; or he could also close his fist and hide the flame — then when he opened it, the fire would still be there.

blocks.name: Hide Wealth
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Days), Rank Initiate, Runes [Earth-like glyph, Illusion-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon an inanimate bundle of goods, with an ENC no greater than its Intensity. It causes the target to become invisible to everyone except the caster or to anyone watching as the spell is cast. Objects and magics hidden with this spell cannot be sensed, even with detection spells, but the presence of the spell of Hide Wealth itself could be. If the hidden goods are moved, the spell dissipates.

blocks.name: Hie Wagon
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: Hie Wagon must be cast on a moving wheeled vehicle, such as a cart, wagon or chariot in which the caster is travelling. It increases the entire vehicle's Movement Rate, including that of its draft animals, by the Intensity of the spell. Animals drawing the wagon return to normal speed if cut loose.

blocks.name: Hold Course
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Days), Rank Initiate, Runes [Movement glyph, Water glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a ship upon which the caster is travelling. For the duration of the spell the ship continues on the exact same course in which it was set when the spell was cast. No directional changes can be made to the ship's course unless Hold Course is allowed to lapse or is dispelled. Likewise natural ocean going events cannot alter its course, although magic of a higher Magnitude can temporarily overwhelm it.

blocks.name: Hurling
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: All objects thrown by the caster have their range multiplied by the Intensity of the spell. The multiple affects all aspects of range, so a thrown javelin under the influence of an Intensity 5 Hurling would have a Close Range of 50m, Effective Range of 100m and Long Range of 250m. The spell does not increase range for purely projectile weapons such as bows and crossbows, but has a partial effect on blowguns and slings, halving its effect.

blocks.name: Identify Scent
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Air glyph, Beast-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell enables the caster to perfectly identify the nature of any familiar scent he encounters while under the spell's influence. It does not give range or direction, just nature. If the scent is of a substance or entity that the caster has never before encountered, he only knows that an unknown scent has been encountered.

blocks.name: Illusory Motion, Odor, Sight, Sound, Substance, Taste
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Resist (Special), Runes [Illusion-like glyph]
blocks.text: These spells have been amalgamated and replaced with Illusion (RuneQuest page 271).

blocks.name: Impede Chaos
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Impede Chaos protects against the attacks and spells of chaotic beings. The spell imposes one additional difficulty grade against any attempt a chaos creature makes to harm the recipient. It has no effect on non-chaotic opponents.

blocks.name: Incarnate Ancestor
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Special), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [Man-like glyph, Spirit-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell summons the spirit of an ancestor to occupy the body of one of its descendants. The family member must be willing, and may be the caster. The summoned spirit will know all spells, knowledge and possible skills which it knew while still living. The spirit of the host body is temporarily suspended in the Spirit Plane and will return when the ancestor decides to leave or is exorcised. Friendly spirits leave when the job at hand is complete; neutral spirits must be bribed to do so using something like Gift Power or Gift Spell, whilst malign ancestors do not leave voluntarily, tying up points of the caster's Devotional Pool until it can be gotten rid of. This spell can summon a specific ancestor back a number of generations up to half the Intensity of the spell. Or it can be cast without naming the specific ancestor desired, in which case a random ancestor comes — a dangerous procedure. Note that powerful ancestors often have a POW Characteristic in excess of normal species maximums.

The note below the table says the Rune Spells value is the number of different Rune Spells known, which are cast with their own Magic Points.
If progressive Folk Magic is used, the Intensity of known spells is counted against the Folk Magic value; a Healing 3 would count as three points, for example.

blocks.name: Increase Temperature
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph]
blocks.text: Increase Temperature raises the temperature by 1d30 Centigrade per point of Intensity, with the possible result of inducing heat exposure to those within the area (RuneQuest page 129). If the temperature rises above freezing point, any ice or snow in the area of effect will begin to melt.

blocks.name: Increase Wind
blocks.stat_line: Area (Hundreds of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Storm glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: This spell increases the average wind STR by 1d10 per point of Intensity for the duration of the spell (RuneQuest page 130). This spell has no effect upon temperature or wind direction.

blocks.name: Induce Uzdo
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is used when a troll woman wishes to give birth to a Great Troll. It summons one of Karrg's followers, an Intensity 4 darkness spirit with a POW of 1d6+24, INT of 2d6+6, CHA of 2d6+6 and Darkness Rune affinity of 100%. The female troll then steps forward and the spirit envelopes her in its form, initiating Spirit Combat. If she manages to inflict damage on the spirit it ceases its attack out of respect for her strength and joins with her. Several hours later, she emerges from the darkness and the spirit returns to the underworld. The troll woman is now pregnant, and gives birth a year later to an uzdo infant. Those that fail to overcome Karrg's follower even once in Spirit Combat before running out of Magic Points have their soul Sundered from their body and die (RuneQuest page 212).

blocks.name: Ingest Scroll
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Devotee, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell is cast on text that the caster holds in his hands or is touching. This could be a scroll, the open pages of a book, an inscription carved in wood or stone and so on. It causes the information to be translated and transferred to the caster's mind, destroying any trace of it on the page or scroll, or crumbling the surface if carved into more permanent material. The wash of knowledge is so overwhelming that the caster must succeed in a roll against his Willpower or go into shock for 1d6 rounds; after which the caster must make and pass a second roll to prevent losing all the information gained.

blocks.name: Insect Song
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Kilometres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: The recipient can, at will, create a loud chirping, rasping noise, which is deafening close at hand and clearly audible up to its range in km away. This noise can be used to transmit messages to all cult initiates within earshot. The message is not understandable to anyone else.

blocks.name: Invigorate
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Invigorate restores the Fatigue Level of the target, up one level per 2 points of Intensity in the spell. This only has an effect upon Fatigue lost to tiredness or effort, not levels lost to blood loss or the like.

blocks.name: Jabbers
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [Beast-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: This spell turns one of the recipient's arms, or optionally tail, in which case the tail cannot use its normal attack such as inject poison, into a jabber, a long bony point used for fighting. It has a weapon Size of Medium, Reach of Long, can impale, Armour Points equal to the spell's Intensity and does 1d10 damage plus any Damage Modifier. The recipient can attack and parry with the jabber using their Unarmed skill.

blocks.name: Keenclaw
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [Beast-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: Both the recipient's hands become paws as per a lion (RuneQuest page 369), doing 1d8 damage plus his Damage Modifier and using his normal Unarmed skill to strike. The magically hard claws are not only capable of inflicting the Bleed and Flurry special effects, but the recipient can use Sunder to tear away hide or armour. The claws are clumsy and cannot hold weapons or tools.

blocks.name: Knowledge
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [unclear]
blocks.text: This spell allows the user to read the past history of an item. If the information cannot be assimilated fully within the duration of the spell, parts may be lost. If the item has a long history, the spell starts at the beginning unless the user dictates a particular time at which to start.

blocks.name: Lava Spear
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph, Earth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a spear. It permits the wielder to fire splatters of molten rock from the tip of the spear by expending magic points. For every Magic Point spent, a gout of lava flies out, inflicting 5d6 damage on impact. Armour protects, but shields which parry the lava are subject to its burning damage. The lava has a range of 100 metres, and uses the wielder's spear combat style to hit.

blocks.name: Leap
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Special), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Movement glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: Leap allows the caster to travel up to 10 times the Intensity in kilometres, by leaping in an arc through the Otherworld to any destination he or she desires. Multiple castings can be combined together to allow additional followers touching the caster to be brought along. The landing place must be known in advance and the trip, no matter its distance, takes a full day.

blocks.name: Leaping Legs
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: When cast upon a leaping insect this increases its leaping ability by 10m per point of Intensity either horizontally or vertically. When cast upon a non-insect, it turns his hind legs into enormous, jointed, bug-like limbs, allowing the recipient to leap half the same distance.

blocks.name: Lie
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Illusion-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is undetectably cast at the same time that the caster tells a lie. Anyone hearing the lie who fails to resist automatically believes it to be true, no matter how outrageous, and continues to believe it until they have incontrovertible evidence of its falsehood. For instance, a trickster used this spell to tell his friends that the Sun wasn't going to rise tomorrow; it would elicit a great deal of panic until next morning or until a Yelm priest performed Divination and asked his deity what had happened.

blocks.name: Lightning
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Runes [Storm glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Lightning miracle (RuneQuest page 272).

blocks.name: Lion's Head
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell begins at the bottom of the page and continues on the next page.

uncertain_readings: Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.
Lion's Head text continues on page 102.

blocks.name: Lion's Head
blocks.text: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a lion. He gains a Bite attack as per a lion (RuneQuest page 369), doing 1d10 damage plus his Damage Modifier. This bite attack can be used in addition to normal weapon attacks and parries, provided the opponent is within reach.

blocks.name: Living Blade
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [Death-like glyph, Movement glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: A recipient under the influence of this spell move with lightning speed in combat, gaining 1 additional Action Point per Round.

blocks.name: Lock
blocks.text: This spell has been renamed to Seal (page 109) to avoid confusion with the Folk Magic spell of the same name.

blocks.name: Locust's Head
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: The recipient's head transforms into that of a giant locust, gaining a number of points of natural armour equal to half the Intensity of the spell, replacing any previous natural armour if greater. This transmogrification usually displaces any helmet not designed for an insectile head of that size. In addition, the recipient gains the ability to chew up and digest almost anything, providing it is not immediately destructive, such as molten metal or glass.

blocks.name: Madness
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Days), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Moon-like glyph]
blocks.text: As per the default Madness miracle (RuneQuest page 272), save that the result of the opposed roll has a more significant effect. If the target is overcome, refer to the Madness table to judge the results of their Willpower roll.

Victim will gibber and act in an insane manner as per the default description.
Victim collapses into a catatonic state.

blocks.name: Magical Arms
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: Each use of the spell creates a magical limb, usually an arm, attached to the torso of the caster. The formed limb is fully capable as per a normal humanoid arm or leg in terms of supporting weight or manipulating objects. This spell can be used to temporarily replace a missing limb or add additional ones beyond the norm. However, the extra limbs do not grant any bonus Action Points; nor, being magical creations, do they count as physical Hit Locations and therefore have no Hit Points.

blocks.name: Magnify Command
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Days), Ranged (Kilometres), Rank Devotee, Runes [Beast-like glyph, other glyphs visible]
blocks.text: Magnify Command must be stacked with a Command spell (page 83). It boosts the duration and range beyond those of the base spell, and forces the commanded creature to suffer a penalty of one Difficulty Grade when resisting the spell.

blocks.name: Mating Ritual
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Chaos-like glyph, Beast-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is normally cast by a female creature of Chaos upon a single male of any humanoid species. The spell allows the two to mate, although the fertility of the union is not guaranteed. The ritual need only last a single hour to achieve its purpose, but the 'wife' of the captive usually makes full use of the entire duration. The ritual inevitably ends in the painful death of the 'husband', whether through wounds, terror or raw exhaustion. When the 'husband' is a human, the viability of the union is almost assured, resulting in pregnancy if the caster can roll equal or less than the man's Endurance. With other races the chances are poorer, a roll suffering one Difficulty Grade, dwarves two grades and three grades for an elf. A successful pregnancy results in a birth or laying of a single egg. The emerging offspring is always female and bears some resemblance to her father, but does not deviate far from the form of her mother.

blocks.name: Matrix Creation
blocks.text: All spell matrix enchantments are now created by performing cult specific Heroquests to the other world.

Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.

blocks.name: Meld Form
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Metres), Rank Devotee, Resist (Special), Runes [Chaos-like glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: This day-long ritual must be undergone to make the spell Proteus effectual (see page 105). A living physical creature of the type desired by the supplicant must be provided, although its SIZ cannot exceed three times the Intensity of the spell. When the ritual ends, the creature vanishes completely, and the supplicant forever-after gains the ability to turn into that creature by use of the Proteus spell. If the sacrificed creature is sapient, it gains a chance to resist the magic using its Willpower. If this fails, the creature's soul is not destroyed, and goes to whatever afterlife it deserves when its body vanishes. Non-sapient creatures, those with INS, cannot resist.

blocks.name: Mind Blast
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Devotee, Runes [Moon-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Mindblast miracle (RuneQuest page 272).

blocks.name: Mindlink
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Devotee, Runes All
blocks.text: Use the default Mindlink miracle (RuneQuest page 272). Shared spells require both users to possess the required Rune Affinity.

blocks.name: Mind-Read
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Illusion-like glyph]
blocks.text: If not resisted, this spell gives the caster the ability to read the conscious thoughts of a target without his consent, although he will know his mind is being read. It cannot be used to trawl through the victim's memories - only their current surface thoughts.

blocks.name: Minor Infection
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Disease/Chaos-like glyph, Death-like glyph]
blocks.text: The caster can infect a target with one of the many minor diseases known to the cult. Those that fail to resist the magic must compare the result of their Endurance roll on the Infection Severity table to judge how serious their infection is, then roll on the Minor Infection table if a random disease is required. All the diseases are described in the boxed text on page 143.

rows.severity: Severity is Chronic, effect occurs each week.
Severity is Acute, effect occurs each day.
Severity is Terminal, effect occurs each hour.
Victim becomes an asymptomatic carrier of the disease.

blocks.title: Infection Type Table
blocks.notes: The row for 51-60 is obscured by scan damage.

blocks.name: Mist Cloud
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Evade), Runes [Storm glyph, Water glyph]
blocks.text: This spell creates a small area of natural-looking mist around a target, which if hostile, may try to resist. Failure means that the cloud will follow the target. Visibility in or through the mist is limited to arm's length, effectively blinding the recipient if they possess no other senses to help.

blocks.name: Mold Rock
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Devotee, Runes [Earth-like glyph, Stasis-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to use his or her hands like hammers, chisels and files, to shape rock. Mold Rock can also be used to dig tunnels or undermine foundations. Shaping Truestone or Adamant is impossible, unless the caster has some form of cult gift or Feat which permits him to do this.

blocks.name: Morale
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Rank Devotee, Runes [Death-like glyph]
blocks.text: Establishes a field around the caster which will affect the entire military regiment or members of the same cult with an unbreakable esprit de corps. Not only are the recipients immune to fear of any kind, automatically winning any Morale Test if using the battle rules from Ships and Shield Walls, but they may also augment their Combat Style by raising it by one Difficulty Grade. Thus a regiment of Humakti swordsmen unaffected by any situational penalties raise their combat skills from Standard to Easy.

uncertain_readings: Infection Type Table has one unreadable row.
Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.

blocks.name: Moth's Head
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: Whenever this spell is cast, the recipient's head becomes identical to that of a large furry moth, with long feathery antennae. Using these sensitive receptors he gains a Track skill equal to the value of the Rune Affinity used to cast the spell. In addition the recipient may automatically pinpoint the exact direction, number and distance of living organisms when within a distance of 10 times the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Moulder
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Plant-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes a mushroom, mildew, mould, slime, or other fungus infection to infest everything within the area. The mould affects inanimate material such as wood, brick, stone, plants and even cloth, by reducing its Hit Points by an amount equal to the Intensity of the spell, crumbling it away. Thus a rope could be eaten through entirely, or a wooden door weakened by rot. Living creatures caught within the area suffer no ill effects from being infested, although their clothes and equipment might.

blocks.name: Multispell
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [Movement-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: This magic allows multiple Folk Magic spells to be cast simultaneously with the Multispell, saving the caster vital actions during times of combat. One Folk Magic spell can be cast for each four points of the Multispell's Intensity. It does not reduce the Magic Point costs of the spells, merely allows them to all be cast at the same moment as the Multispell, costing 1 Action Point, whether or not they are progressive. The only restriction is that duplicate Folk Magic spells cannot be cast multiple times on the same target. For example an initiate who cast Multispell at Intensity 9 could on the same Turn decide to simultaneously cast a Bladesharp 2 on himself and fire off two Disruption 3 spells, the latter of which must strike separate targets. This would cost a punitive 8 Magic Points, but might well take his foes by surprise!

blocks.name: Oath
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Illusion-like glyph]
blocks.text: Binds two or more people in physical contact into a mutually sworn pact, backed up by the threat of divine retribution. The maximum number of participants is equal to the Intensity of the spell, of whom the caster may or may not include himself. Oath is always cast in concert with a second spell, whose effect is held in check by the cult deity. If thereafter anyone breaks the oath, they are immediately subject to the spell at double the caster's relevant Rune Affinity. For example, an Oath spell cast by an Initiate of Humakt would be combined with Sever Spirit, promising a swift death for oath breakers. If the caster had a Death Rune affinity of 75%, then whoever broke his word would be subject to a Sever Spirit spell cast at 150% (double the Rune Affinity for the embedded retributive spell). Although the duration of the oath depends on the terms sworn, the Devotional Pool cost of both the Oath and its additional spell are not considered enduring and can be recovered normally.

blocks.name: Pain Blow
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Death-like glyph, Chaos-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast on a weapon. It causes any hit that penetrates armour to potentially incapacitate the location struck in addition to normal damage. The effect can be resisted by the victim, provided they win an opposed test of their Endurance against the attack roll of the wielder. If the damage resulted in a Serious Wound or worse, that Hit Location is automatically incapacitated.

blocks.name: Pain Tooth
blocks.text: Use Pain Blow above, but natural weapons can be affected also.

blocks.name: Panic
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Darkness glyph, Chaos-like glyph]
blocks.text: If the target fails to resist, they are seized with an overpowering terror and cannot cast any spells or attack in melee, though he can parry or evade normally. Usually, the victim runs away.

uncertain_readings: Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.

blocks.name: Passage
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Months), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Stasis-like glyph, Communication-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be stacked with Lock, or with Seal (page 109). Each point of Intensity allows one additional person to pass through or open the magically sealed aperture besides the caster. It is cast on the object or portal whilst the additional persons are present, laying their hands on the opening as the spell is intoned. These additional persons cannot boost the priest's Lock spell with their magic points unless in Mindlink with him.

blocks.name: Path Watch
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Movement glyph, Communication-like glyph]
blocks.text: Used when passing through dangerous or questionable territory, it must be laid upon a known path or a visible road which will be travelled by the caster. Whilst the caster remains on that path, the spell will alert him of the presence of all traps and enemies, though not their type, within the radius of effect.

blocks.name: Pathway
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to determine the direction of the nearest oasis. For every two points of Intensity the next nearest oasis can be sensed. Thus if Pathway was cast at Intensity 6, directions to the nearest three oases are known. The spell tells only direction and order of closeness, not the actual distance.

blocks.name: Peace
blocks.stat_line: Area (Hundreds of Kilometres), Cost 3, Duration (Hours), Rank Devotee, Resist (Special), Runes [three vertical strokes glyph, Communication-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes all persons not of Devotee status within the area to lay down their weapons and forget all violence and war. For the length of the spell they prefer rather to listen to the wonders of peace and love which the caster sends ringing through their minds.

blocks.name: Plant Eye/Plant Spy
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Ranged (Kilometres), Runes [Plant-like glyph]
blocks.text: A specially chosen plant may be used as a spy by converting the leaves to sound and touch transmission devices without altering their appearance. Additional plants can be prepared for use with this spell, up to a number equal to the Intensity of the spell. Whilst in use the caster may swap between the chosen plants, but only spy out of one at a time. If any of the specially prepared plants are cut or uprooted, they cease transmitting information.

blocks.name: Plastron
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Ranged (Touch), Runes [Beast-like glyph, Stasis-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell gives the recipient's body a carapace or shell over his chest and abdomen. This natural armour has no effect on Strike Rank or the user's Swim skill, but it does prevent him from wearing any other armour over the torso. In return it grants a number of Armour Points equal to one and a half times the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Power Drain
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Power Drain grants the caster the ability to drain Magic Points in hand to hand combat. The caster's touch will reach through an opponent's armour forcing them to resist in an opposed test of Willpower. If the caster wins, they drain a rolled amount of Magic Points equal to the Intensity of the spell, as per the Earthpower Effect table (page 92). The caster receives none of the drained power however, with all of the Magic Points flowing through them directly to the cult deity instead.

blocks.name: Precision (Specific Skill)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [unclear]
blocks.text: There are many variants of this spell, each one only usable with a specific skill. Thus, there exists Precision Boating, Precision Evade, Precision Stealth and so forth. When the recipient succeeds in the appropriate skill roll, a 1d100 result that would normally be a simple success becomes a critical success. Criticals, failures and fumbles are unchanged.

blocks.name: Predict Weather
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Kilometres), Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [Storm glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: When this spell is cast, the user knows what the natural weather will be like for the following 3 x Intensity hours in the region covered by the magic. Predict Weather cannot account for the actions of gods, demons or the use of weather-affecting spells.

uncertain_readings: The Precision heading is visibly truncated; '(Specific Skill)' is reconstructed from the line and spell text.
Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.

blocks.name: Proteus
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Chaos-like glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to change his shape into that of one creature with which he has undergone the Meld Form ritual (page 102). The user's INT and POW do not change, but all other characteristics become identical to those of the creature used in the Meld Form ritual. The user can also use special innate powers of the creature he has become. For the duration of the spell, the user can transform back and forth between his usual shape and the chosen creature's. Each transformation takes one melee round, during which the caster can do nothing else. If the caster stacks more than one point, he can alternate between his available shapes. Bremildaker has used the Meld Form ritual to gain the ability to turn into a sardine, an octopus and a small sea serpent. If he casts three concurrent incidences of the Proteus spell, he can switch back and forth between his own shape, the sardine's, the octopus' and the sea serpent's. Thus, he could become a sardine, then an octopus, then himself, then the sea serpent, then the octopus again, if he wished, for the spell's duration.

blocks.name: Purification Ritual
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: This night-long ritual is used to cleanse female trolls who have birthed a litter of trollkin. Normally, any female troll who gives birth to trollkin will henceforth give birth to trollkin in all future pregnancies. A female undergoing this ritual and avoiding pregnancy for a full year following the ritual is cleansed and can once again give birth to normal troll children, though there is no guarantee that she will do so.

blocks.name: Purify Water
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Runes [Water glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes all particulate matter and contaminants in a 10 m3 per point of Intensity volume of water to settle. It will work on foreign minerals dissolved in the water, such as salt, including poisons, making the water perfectly transparent and otherwise safe to drink. The spell is often used to make tainted oases or chaos infested swamp water drinkable for men and beasts alike.

blocks.name: Radiate
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Rank Devotee, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph]
blocks.text: This allows the caster's body to radiate intense heat, capable of burning anything which is touching it with 1d6 damage per two points of Intensity in the spell. This radiant heat reduces by 1d6 per metre distance from the body and armour counts against it. Weapons striking the caster must first suffer the burning damage to see if they survive long enough to inflict their own harm. Thus a javelin (3AP/8HP) thrown at someone under the aegis of an Intensity 11 Radiate would have to survive 6d6 damage before it struck, likely causing it to burst into fire and turn to ash before touching the caster.

blocks.name: Rain
blocks.stat_line: Area (Hundreds of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Water glyph]
blocks.text: This spell can only be cast when the sky is overcast or at least 50% clouds. Torrential rain, 1mm per minute, will then fall within the area, reducing visibility, soaking clothes and bowstrings, and potentially turning the ground into a slippery morass. When the spell ends, the rainfall stops.

blocks.name: Raise Ghoul
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Raise Ghoul may be used on any 'fresh' corpse which has lain dead no longer than a number of days equal to half the Intensity of the spell. It recalls the original spirit before it can be judged and sent to the afterlife, binding it back into its own rotted body. The transformed ghoul claws out of the grave or mausoleum the following night, adopting its original Characteristics when still alive, but adjusted as described in the RuneQuest Core Rules (RuneQuest page 355). Despite creating the ghoul, this spell grants the caster no control over the undead creature.

blocks.name: Rebirth of Chaos
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Metres), Rank Devotee, Resist (Special), Runes [Chaos glyph]
blocks.text: When cast on someone who lacks any chaos features, it permits them to roll twice on the Chaotic Features table (page 209) and choose which one they will take. If, on the other hand, the spell is cast on somebody who already has one or more features, it causes the recipient to re-roll all their chaotic features on the table, permanently changing them, plus receive the bonus of gaining an additional feature of their choice. There is a danger in this. The recipient has a percentage chance of dying equal to 5% plus 5% more per chaotic feature he already has. For example, a broo already with two chaos features stands a 15% chance of death if the spell is cast upon him. Unwilling recipients are allowed to resist using Endurance.

uncertain_readings: Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.
Rebirth of Chaos continues onto the next page and is completed there.

blocks.name: Reconstruction
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Rank Devotee, Runes [unclear]
blocks.text: This spell causes a sequence from the past, equal in length to the spell's duration, to replay for the caster's senses, as long as he or she is in the immediate area in which it took place, or is in Mindlink with someone who is currently there. Whilst the spell lasts, the caster is in a trance state and no one else can observe what they sense. The user must state the time and date of starting the vision.

blocks.name: Recover Spirit
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Resist (Special), Runes [Chaos-like glyph, Spirit-like glyph, other glyph visible]
blocks.text: This spell must be used within one day of death, and requires the caster to meet the victim's spirit on the spirit plane and force it back to its body by succeeding in one round of Spirit Combat with the departing spirit. However, after one day the spirit will have departed on the journey to the halls of the dead to await judgement, and the spell will not work. The body must first be healed of any Major Wounds. A fumble results in the caster finding the wrong spirit to put back in the body. Use the Ancestral Summons table on page 99 to determine the replacement spirit found.

blocks.name: Reduce Flame
blocks.text: See Suppress Oakfed (page 117).

blocks.name: Refine Medicine
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph, Plant-like glyph]
blocks.text: This Rune spell is usually utilised in concert with the Find Healing Plants skill, to create an enduring herbal cure for a particular disease, thus saving the recipient of the medicinal powder, paste or whatever from needing to know the Cure Disease spell; however, the Healing skill is still necessary to apply it to an ill victim. Despite the instantaneous nature of the spell, the refined medicine so created lasts for a number of weeks equal to the Intensity it is cast at.

blocks.name: Reflection
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Infinity-like glyph, Movement-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Reflection miracle (RuneQuest page 274).

blocks.name: Regrow Limb
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Special), Rank Devotee, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: This spell regenerates a severed or maimed limb or appendage, taking one week per missing Hit Point to grow back (see Permanent Injuries, RuneQuest page 122). The spell must be maintained until completion, otherwise the partially regenerated limb will shrivel away.

blocks.name: Regrow Organ
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Special), Rank Devotee, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: As per Regrow Limb, save that it affects an organ such as an eye or tongue instead. Due to the smaller size of the area affected, Regrow Organ only takes a week to complete.

blocks.name: Release Intelligence
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Rank Devotee, Resist (Special), Runes [Beast-like glyph, Spirit-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell only affects creatures which possess INS. It is the opposite of the spell Fix Intelligence (page 94), directly converting its INS characteristic to INT. The personality and memories of the creature remain as before, but it gains the ability to reason, learn knowledge and communication skills and use magic. Creatures unwilling to be awakened to sapience may attempt to resist using Willpower. This spell completely cancels the effects of the Fix Intelligence spell.

blocks.name: Remove (Specific Body Part)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Chaos-like glyph, Spirit-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to pull off the specified limb or organ without damage or pain. The detached limb can still be moved and the caster receives normal sensory input from it. Someone with Remove Hand could take off their hand and send it crawling away. Even if the user cannot see his limb, he can still control it and feel what it feels, though he may not be able to tell where or how far away it is. If the separated limb is damaged, all normal rules ensue as if it were not detached, thus the owner can become incapacitated, or

uncertain_readings: Remove (Specific Body Part) continues on the next page.
Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.

blocks.name: Remove (Specific Body Part)
blocks.text: even be poisoned. Replacing the missing part with a spell such as Regrow Limb automatically fails, unless the old part is destroyed. The user can reaffix the detached part if he brings it back within the spell's duration. If the spell ends before it is restored, it must remain separated until he casts the spell again.

blocks.name: Reproduce
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Initiate, Runes [Fertility-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Fecundity miracle (RuneQuest page 270), save that the spell can also affect a plant, for example an apple tree.

blocks.name: Resist Iron Burn
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: When cast upon a member of the Aldryami or Uz race, this spell grants the recipient a brief respite from their vulnerability to Iron weapons. In effect it negates the increased damage of iron weapons against those Elder Races. Resist Iron Burn replaces the original spell of Cure Iron Burn for ease of play and added survivability.

blocks.name: Restore Health
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Harmony-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell restores Characteristic points that have been lost to disease or to the effects of Tapping (see XX). It does not however cure any disease(s) that may be present. The number of Characteristic Points recovered is equal to the Intensity of the spell. Unlike previous versions of this spell, Restore Health is not restricted to a specific Characteristic, but it is recommended that availability is limited to cults possessing the Harmony Rune.

blocks.name: Restore Vision
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Harmony-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell restores normal sight to the target, whether it was lost permanently or simply through the temporary use of a spell such as Blinding. If the eyes themselves were destroyed, a Regrow Organ must be cast on each eye prior to casting Restore Vision.

blocks.name: Resurrect/Resurrection
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Rank Devotee, Runes [Harmony-like glyph, Spirit-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use the default Resurrect miracle (RuneQuest page 274).

blocks.name: Reverse Chaos
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [Chaos glyph]
blocks.text: A target who fails to resist must roll on the detrimental Reverse Chaos table, and suffer the listed effect. Once the spell concludes however, it does not taint its target permanently with Chaos.
