blocks.name: Ride River Horse
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Water glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: When cast at the banks of a watercourse, this spell summons a River Horse, which can be mounted and ridden on a trip from one headwater to anywhere else on that river, journeying through the watery realms of the Otherworld. The caster must have a clear image of where he wishes to travel to, or it will deposit him at some random place. The River Horse may bear as many passengers as the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Ritual of Devouring
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Special), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Chaos-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is ritually cast before killing and devouring a sapient victim. Properly done, this spell enables whoever devours the victim to gain knowledge. Each participant in the grisly feast must eat 1d3 SIZ points of the victim to gain benefits. The eater gains 1d6 percentiles in any skill in which the victim was at least 25 percentiles better than the eater. For each Folk Magic spell the victim knew, the eater may roll his Willpower or less on 1D100. Success allows the eater to gain knowledge of that spell. No higher magic can be obtained through this ritual, unless both the victim and eater share the same cult. The maximum number of participants in the feast cannot exceed the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Ritual of Rebirth
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Chaos-like glyph]
blocks.text: During the spell's duration, any victim eaten by the caster will, a few days later, be laid in an egg. One season after the devouring, the eggs will hatch and the being or beings devoured under the spell will be reborn as a scorpion man, scorpion elf, scorpion baboon, etc., depending on the species originally eaten. The upper torso and head will remain identical to the one devoured, whilst the lower body will now be that of a scorpion. The victim recalls all previous skills, and uses his Unarmed skill to attack with his new tail.

blocks.name: Safe
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Months), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Stasis-like glyph, Darkness-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is cast on a container or opening to bar unwanted passage. When anyone other than the caster, or someone in Mindlink with the caster, tries to open the secured container or cross a doorway with this spell, he is magically attacked. If the victim fails to resist, he takes as much damage as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (worn armour does not protect) to a random Hit Location, and is forced back. If, on the other hand, the target is not overcome, the spell is broken and he may enter freely, as may anyone following. If he fails to overcome the spell on his first try, he can try again and again until he either succeeds or is slain.

uncertain_readings: Some Reverse Chaos continuation rows are degraded but legible enough to transcribe.
The exact Rune glyphs for Safe are visually degraded.

blocks.name: Sanctify
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Devotee, Runes Any
blocks.text: Use the default Consecrate miracle (RuneQuest page 267).

blocks.name: Sapience
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: Sapience grants a bonus to all INT-based skills, raising their chance by one difficulty grade for the duration of the spell. In addition, if progressive Folk Magic is used, it raises its Intensity cap as if the caster's INT had been increased by the Magnitude of the Sapience.

blocks.name: Seal
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Months), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Stasis-like glyph, other glyph]
blocks.text: A more powerful version of the Lock Folk Magic spell, which works on any object or item which can be 'closed'; i.e. a door, box, chest and even a book! Seal provides the shut object with a number of Armour Points equal to the Intensity of the spell. Any tool or weapon used to break it is subject to the rules for Using Weapons Against Inanimate Objects (RuneQuest page 122). Additionally, the caster may open and close the sealed object at will without needing to recast the magic.

blocks.name: Seal Spirit
blocks.text: Use Recover Spirit (page 106), save that the spirit of the deceased must be defeated in the first round of Spirit Combat.

blocks.name: Seal Tongue
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Weeks), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Resist (Special), Runes [Truth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This night-long ritual must be performed over a willing recipient and lasts many months. It helps during interrogations when resisting any truth-detecting or truth-enforcing spell of equal or less Magnitude. Any time the recipient becomes the focus of such magic, they may attempt to resist the magic using their Willpower, whether or not the truth spell normally grants a resistance roll. If the recipient successfully resists, the spell has no effect on him. If he fails however, he dies revealing nothing.

blocks.name: Seal Wound
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Death-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is cast upon a weapon. Damage inflicted by the weapon cannot be cured with magic or chaotically regenerate, but may only recover naturally, using the victim's Healing Rate (RuneQuest page 121). A Heal or stronger spells will still stop blood loss, but do not restore any damage. The skills of First Aid and Healing are not affected by this spell.

blocks.name: Seastrength
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Water glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: This spell doubles the recipient's base STR for the purpose of boosting their Damage Modifier. It has no effect on the value of STR based skills. Thus someone with STR 15 and SIZ 16 would recalculate their Damage Modifier with STR 30 and SIZ 16, increasing it from +1d4 to +1d10. The target must be in contact with seawater when the spell is cast.

blocks.name: Second Mouth
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Stasis-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell may only be cast upon a woman. It renders her practically immune to rape, by permitting her to take immediate revenge upon the assailant; incidentally inflicting damage to the rapist's abdomen as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table. If this results in a Serious Wound or worse, the member is bitten clean off.

blocks.name: Sever Spirit
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Rank Devotee, Runes [Death glyph]
blocks.text: Use the Sever Spirit miracle (RuneQuest page 275).

blocks.name: Shadows Dance
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Hours), Rank Devotee, Runes [Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This spell requires the recipients to dance in a great ceremony for at least an hour prior to receiving its effect. All participants in the dance receive a special benefit increasing the damage they inflict in Spirit Combat to that

uncertain_readings: Seal Tongue duration appears as Weeks in the stat line, while body text says it lasts many months.

blocks.name: Shadows Dance
blocks.text: indicated by referencing each individual's Darkness Rune affinity on the Spirit Damage table (RuneQuest page 201).

blocks.name: Shake Earth
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Initiate, Runes [Earth glyph]
blocks.text: Use the Earthquake miracle (RuneQuest page 268).

blocks.name: Shape Metal
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Devotee, Runes [Stasis-like glyph, Earth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the user to use his hands like hammers, files and drills to shape metal. It is equally usable with any and all Rune metals. If the metal is hot, the user will take no damage.

blocks.name: Shattering
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Truth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell acts as a powerfully augmented Disruption spell. If the target fails to resist, it inflicts 1d3 damage plus an additional bonus equal to the Intensity of the spell. Thus a Shattering cast at Intensity 8 would inflict 1d3+8 damage to a single Hit Location.

blocks.name: Shield
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Stasis-like glyph, Movement-like glyph]
blocks.text: Each point of this spell serves as either 1 point of physical or magical protection, as desired by the caster. Physical defence counts as Armour Points, whilst the magical defence blocks spells of an equivalent numerical Magnitude and lower. Thus a Shield spell cast at Intensity 7 could provide 7AP, or 7 points of magical defence, or a combination thereof. The physical protection provided does not stack atop worn armour, rather it supplants it in those areas less protected, although it will work against certain spells, those that normally bypass natural and worn armour, such as Lightning.

blocks.name: Shield of Darkness
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: As per Shield, but adds the darkening effect of the Folk Magic spell Darkwall (page 64).

blocks.name: Shooting Star
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a single arrow. When fired it starts to burn, leaving a glowing trail behind, and splits into as many arrows as the Intensity of the spell, each one targeting a different victim. The caster makes a single attack roll using their relevant Combat Style, applying the result to all the victims simultaneously. The Hit Location however is rolled separately for each target. The arrows are treated as if under the effects of a Firearrow spell, but cannot provide a lingering penalty if used to impale, as they are consumed by the flames.

blocks.name: Sidana's Blessing
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Months), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Chaos-like glyph, Fertility-like glyph]
blocks.text: Similar to Reproduce, this spell when cast on a single living creature of any race, permits the caster to ensure it will bear or sire offspring through sexual congress. If the union is incestuous, the spell will succeed to the greatest degree possible, either producing twins or permitting the Characteristics of a sole offspring to be increased. In the latter case, one die per Characteristic may be exchanged for its maximum value. For example a human offspring would replace the 3d6 roll for STR with 2d6+6, and so on with the other Characteristics. To be successful, Sidana's Blessing must be maintained for the entire pregnancy. The spell does not protect a female broo from the usual consequences of broo birth.

blocks.name: Silence/Silence Sphere
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower)
blocks.text: This spell creates a barrier to sound around the perimeter of its area, which is centred upon the target. No sound can pass this barrier. Characters inside can hear each other, but can hear nothing from outside, and vice versa. If the spell is cast at an unwilling or unknowing enemy, they may resist.

blocks.name: Silver Track
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph, Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell calls upon the stars to illuminate a path or set of tracks the caster wishes to follow. Providing at least one star is visible in the sky, the path shows up in the caster's vision with a faint silvery glitter, doubling her Track skill and permitting her to move at full speed if the night sky is clear, or half speed if somewhat overcast.

uncertain_readings: Shooting Star rune glyphs are present but not safely normalised.
The Sidana spelling is visually clear, but deity/cult context is not provided on this page.

blocks.name: Skin Broo
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Months), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Resist (Special), Runes [Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This two-stage magic spell begins with a ritual that may only be cast on a cult holy night and lasts up to a number of hours equal to the spell's Intensity. A broo captive is brought before the caster, who must torture, slay and skin it, then use its skin to make a drum. This magical instrument can only be activated once per night, anytime after sunset. Whilst the drum is playing, any broo within spell range who can hear it will be Demoralized as the Folk Magic spell, towards whatever the caster decided when fashioning the drum: darkness, trolls, etc. The broo can resist by winning an opposed test of their Willpower against the Musicianship skill of the playing caster. The spell must be maintained by the caster to preserve the drum's enchantment, but once the duration expires, the skin rots away to a putrid mess and the captive soul of the sacrificed broo will be released as a disease spirit.

blocks.name: Sky Crater Fury
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Moon glyph]
blocks.text: This spell increases the caster's chance to attack with weapons by one Difficulty Grade. Thus a Lunar warrior unfettered by any other Situational Modifiers would normally have an attack chance of Standard, but under this spell it would be raised to Easy. Conversely the caster suffers a penalty of one Difficulty Grade to any parry or evade he attempts. Additionally the user cannot cast magic other than attack spells: Disruption, Smother, Mindblast and so on.

blocks.name: Slash
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death glyph, Stasis-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon an axe. It provides a bonus to the axe damage as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (page 109).

blocks.name: Smoldering Rebellion
blocks.text: This spell has not been converted due to its abstraction and difficulty of use in play.

blocks.name: Snow
blocks.stat_line: Area (Hundreds of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Air/Storm-like glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast when precipitation is present. If it is raining, the spell changes all the rain within the spell's radius of effect to snow, sleet or hail as decided by the Gamemaster. If it is already snowing, the spell doubles the amount of snow falling.

blocks.name: Solace
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Harmony-like glyph]
blocks.text: When this spell is cast, it acts to cancel mental distress from everyone within the area of effect, whether from grief over the loss of a loved one, from fear of death or whatever. The effect is to relieve stress on the victim and enable him or her to go about normal actions. It does not relieve pain or incapacitation, such as from wounds, but it does enable them to face the prospect of death bravely.

blocks.name: Soul Sight
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Runes [Spirit-like glyph, Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use the Soul Sight miracle (RuneQuest page 275).

blocks.name: Soulfire
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph, Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: Soulfire augments any Disruption spells cast by the recipient, adding +1 to damage for every two points of Intensity. So under an Intensity 7 Soulfire, the damage of Disruption spell is increased to 1d3+4.

blocks.name: Spawn Crawling Hand
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Weeks), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Chaos-like glyph]
blocks.text: This ritual must be performed upon a deceased humanoid creature tied to the Man Rune. It detaches and animates one of the hands of the victim, irrevocably tainting it with Chaos. The hand gains a fanged mouth at the wrist and the ability to scuttle along on its fingers at a Movement Rate of 2. Until the spell expires, it can survive indefinitely if provided proper food, usually gorp and other spawn. The hand responds to spoken commands of its creator only, allowing it to be used as a messenger, spy or crawling assassin. The hand has STR of 1d3, SIZ of 1d2, 1 Hit Point and uses the Athletics, Brawn, Endurance, Evade and Unarmed skills of the deceased corpse. It is capable of leaping up to its STR in metres, and if it manages to grab the throat of someone it is sent to kill, it strangles with whatever Damage Modifier it had before the hand was detached.

uncertain_readings: The rune glyphs on Snow are visibly degraded.
The word 'spawn' in Spawn Crawling Hand's food sentence is visually likely but contextually odd.

blocks.name: Speak to/with (Specific Creatures)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph], any elemental
blocks.text: This spell has many different cult specific variations such as Speak to Birds, Speak to Herd Beast, Speak with Insects, Speak to Scorpion, Speak with Wolves, etc. It allows the recipient to talk with one type of creature for the length of the spell. Deceit or Influence can be used if the creature needs convincing. The spell does not instill any intelligence to the creature, so it can speak only from its natural awareness.

blocks.name: Special Lock
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Months), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Stasis-like glyph, Communication-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be combined with Seal. When cast on a specific person, it allows the recipient to freely open the object or portal affected by the Seal spell.

blocks.name: Spell Teaching
blocks.text: This spell has been made superfluous by the normal process of learning Folk Magic from a cult or mentor (RuneQuest page 177). However, this still requires a week of ritual and training in a cult holy place during which time nothing else may be done except for eating and sleeping.

blocks.name: Spell Trading
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Communication-like glyph, Trade-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster the ability to trade one use of any Rune spell which they currently know, even spells that did not originate from their cult, in exchange for one use of any Rune spell known by an initiate or devotee of another cult. The trade must be voluntarily and mutually agreeable. Trading counts the same as casting the spell and the originator loses the requisite number of Magic Points from their Devotional Pool, which remain out of use until the person the spell was traded to eventually casts it. This spell cannot be used to trade another instance of Spell Trading. The Intensity and Magnitude of proffered Rune spells remain those of the original donator, however the values cannot exceed the Intensity of the Spell Trading used to exchange them. Spell Trading may only be used within the boundaries of a Create Market spell (page 87) and requires the symbolic passing of a token representing the magic. Thus a Shield spell would use a shield, a Humakt spell a knife, and a Summon Sylph a bag of air; whether someone will trade a particular Rune spell depends on the relationship of his cult to the trader, trust of that merchant and possible conflict of Runic associations.

blocks.name: Spider Bite
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a gigantic arachnid. He gains Armour Points equal to half the Intensity of the spell on his head and a bite attack capable of inflicting 1d6 damage, plus any Damage Bonus. Bite attacks are Size M, Range S, and use the recipient's Unarmed skill to strike. If this was not bad enough, the bite injects venom with a Potency equal to the caster's Rune Affinity used to cast the spell, but otherwise has the same properties as Giant Spider venom (RuneQuest page 388).

blocks.name: Spider Limbs
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This spell sprouts four extra limbs from the user's sides. One pair are extra legs, used to double the user's Movement Rate and granting the Adhering creature ability (RuneQuest page 312). The other pair are arms, with tiny claws, too small to hold any weapon but a net, which may be wielded at the user's normal skill if part of one of their Combat Styles, or may grapple instead. The user may simultaneously use other weapons in his 'real' arms. In effect, the recipient receives one free grapple or net attack per round, in addition to their normal combat actions. When a hit is scored against one of the recipient's limbs, a second dice roll should be made to see if it is their normal limb (high), or his additional spider one (low). The spider limbs have the same Hit Points as their contemporary one, but have natural chitin armour equal to half the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Spider's Head
blocks.text: See Spider Bite (page 112).

blocks.name: Spirit Block
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Runes [Spirit-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use the Spirit Block miracle (RuneQuest page 276), with the proviso that this spell does not block the Spirits of Reprisal of the cult that provides it.

uncertain_readings: The exact second rune glyph for Spell Trading is visually uncertain.
Spider Bite uses 'Range S' in the scan; this may be intended as Reach S.

blocks.name: Spirit Guardian
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Days), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Spirit-like glyph, Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a friendly and willing ancestral spirit. It sets up a mental connection, identical to Mindlink, between spirit and caster, allowing them to share magic and communicate freely. Until the spell ends, the spirit guardian will follow the caster about, protecting him from malicious spirit attacks in the Otherworld, or manifesting in the physical world when necessary. The largest Intensity of spirit which can be requested to act as a guardian is equal to half the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Spirit Melding
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Spirit-like glyph, Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a friendly spirit, requesting it to envelop the caster. Whilst melded, the spirit suffers any Magic Point loss the caster should have taken due to Spirit Combat. If this reduces the spirit to zero, it suffers the consequences of defeat, although the caster may release it from the spell before it reaches that stage. The largest Intensity of spirit which can be melded with is equal to half the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Sprout
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Plant-like glyph]
blocks.text: This causes a seed to immediately quicken and send forth roots. Over the course of the duration, the infant plant grows and expands as if one year had passed for every minute the spell lasts. This usually results in a mature sapling or bush with fully developed foliage. This spell affects any mostly intact seed, even those hundreds of years old, provided it has been protected against insects, fungus and extremes of temperature.

blocks.name: Sprout Arms
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Runes [Beast-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes two additional arms to sprout from the recipient's sides. This grants them 1 additional Action Point and permits the carrying of additional weapons or shields. The new arms have the same Hit Points as the user's normal ones, but lack any armour save what the recipient naturally possesses due to the thickness of hide, scales and so on. Any hit on an arm location should roll again to see if the blow lands on the original arm (high) or the additional one (low).

blocks.name: Sprout Legs
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes an additional pair of legs to sprout from the sides of the recipient. The leg is usable as a normal appendage, and has the same Hit Points and natural armour as the other legs. Each additional pair adds 2m to the Movement Rate of the recipient, and provides additional stability if one or more of the original limbs have been rendered inoperable due to Serious Wounds.

blocks.name: Sprout Tail
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell adds an additional tail alongside the original, if the recipient has one. Depending on its form, the tail usable as a normal appendage, and has the same Hit Points and natural armour as the other tail, or leg if no tail exists. The tail can be utilised in combat using the recipient's Unarmed skill, but unless it is a creature with a naturally dangerous tail, like a scorpionman, it will be limited to 1d3 damage and special effects such as Trip Opponent or Bash.

blocks.name: Sprout Wings
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Runes [Beast-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell grows a pair of very fast beating membranous wings from the recipient's back, allowing him to fly at a Movement Rate of 12m. Treat the wings as having the same Hit Points as the recipient's legs, but no Armour Points. Becoming over encumbered when flying will accumulate fatigue and slow down this speed as normal (RuneQuest page 116).

blocks.name: Star Ward
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell resembles Warding, but instead of wands or totems, it uses pieces of smooth silver. It does 1d3 damage per two points of Intensity to incoming enemies who cross the perimeter. In addition, it acts as a Shimmer spell against incoming missiles. Unlike Warding, Star Ward provides no magic protection. The area inside the ward is bathed in a soft silver glow, so the area affected is always illuminated as if by a Light spell.

uncertain_readings: Sprout Tail appears to omit 'is' in the phrase 'the tail usable as a normal appendage'; preserved as close to source wording.
Star Ward stat line has no visible Cost value on this page image.

blocks.name: Stinger
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell transforms the abdomen or hind-quarters of the recipient into that of a giant bee, complete with sting and natural armour to half the Intensity of the spell. The stinger can be used in combat using the Unarmed skill, has Size M, Reach M, does 1d8 damage and injects venom with a Potency equal to the caster's Rune Affinity used to cast the spell. This causes the Agony condition in the Hit Location stung.

blocks.name: Stone-Biting
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This spell strengthens the teeth of the recipient, permitting them to chew through inanimate objects without breaking their dentition (RuneQuest page 123). It allows any bite attack to ignore a number of Armour Points equal to the Intensity of the spell and has several practical uses. Firstly it allows the user to chew through bonds, or even slowly excavate stone tunnels. It can also be used in combat against heavily armoured foes, providing the user has suitable bite damage.

blocks.name: Stones to Kill
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Days), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Stasis-like glyph]
blocks.text: This night-long ritual enchants a number of black rocks equal to the Intensity of the spell. When thrown at a Chaotic target that is hit, they take effect. The stones need not penetrate armour, and the target cannot resist the effect. Each stone is one-use and will cancel out a single chaos feature of the struck target for 1d6 hours. If the target has more than one chaos feature, the one affected is determined randomly. As part of the ritual, the caster must eat the heart of a dead troll.

blocks.name: Strength of Basmol
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell swells the recipient's sinews and muscles, granting a leonine physique. His current Damage Modifier is increased by one step for each two points of Intensity.

blocks.name: Strike
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death glyph, Truth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is cast upon a weapon. The next blow by that weapon, provided it is not parried, ignores all armour, both natural and worn.

blocks.name: Strongblade
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death glyph]
blocks.text: This spell increases the Armour Points of a sword or dagger blade to a value equal to the Intensity of the spell. In addition it suffers no damage when striking inanimate objects (RuneQuest page 123).

blocks.name: Strongnet
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Plant-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a net, making it nigh unbreakable by normal means, including fire, effectively increasing its Armour Points to a value equal to the Intensity of the spell. Someone trapped in the net must escape by unwrapping itself, unless they are immensely strong enough to tear it.

blocks.name: Submerge
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Special), Runes [Water glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: Each point of Intensity drags 10 SIZ of a floating creature, or its totalled corresponding mass on the Creature Size Equivalency table (RuneQuest page 327) if an inanimate object, under the surface of the water. If swamped, naturally non-buoyant objects will sink to the bottom. Unwilling creatures targeted by the spell may attempt a Swim roll to resist.

blocks.name: Summon (Specific Species)
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Kilometres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Special), Runes [Beast-like glyph]
blocks.text: Summons a particular species of creature deemed sacred to the cult to the presence of the caster. The total SIZ of creatures so affected must be equal or less than 20 times the Intensity of the spell. Thus an initiate of Basmol who casts Summon Lion at Intensity 7 would draw 140 SIZ worth of lions; about six of the beasts if average sized. The creatures must travel to the caster under their own steam, resulting in

uncertain_readings: Summon (Specific Species) continues on the next page.
Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.

blocks.name: Summon (Specific Species)
blocks.text: their arrival part way through the spell's duration. Neither are they under the caster's control once they do arrive, which may result in unpleasantness, depending on their nature. Creatures under control of others may attempt to resist the summoning, but in this case it is the rider, herder and so on, who must use an appropriate skill in the opposed test. A rider of a summoned beast would use their Ride skill against the casting roll for instance.

blocks.name: Summon Ancestor
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Spirit-like glyph, Man-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell summons a randomly determined spirit from the Land of the Dead, as rolled on the Ancestral Summons table (page 99). Providing it is friendly, the caster can request a brief service from it as long as can be attempted before the summoning concludes. This usually entails entering Spirit Combat with a foe, or casting its magic in support. Neutral ancestors may need to be convinced by overcoming it in an opposed test of the caster's Customs or Influence against the spirit's Willpower. Hostile ancestors generally make a nuisance of themselves and provide sarcastic commentary about the ineptitude of the caster.

blocks.name: Summon Dead
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This ritual must be performed over the corpse, grave or other remains of the individual being summoned. The name of the individual must also be known. If the person being contacted presently resides within Ty Kora Tek's vast Caverns of the Dead, his ghost rises from the earth at the end of the summoning, patiently waiting to speak with the summoner or be given a task to perform. In the latter case the ghost is treated as a haunt (RuneQuest page 406).

blocks.name: Summon Elemental
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Rank Initiate, Runes Any Elemental
blocks.text: The spells of Summon Small, Medium and Large Elemental have been supplanted. Use the Elemental Summoning miracle (RuneQuest page 269).

blocks.name: Summon Founder
blocks.text: The Founders are now treated as greater spirits and can only be summoned via Spirit Magic. See Cult of Waha (page XX).

blocks.name: Summon Guardian
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: As per Summon Ancestor, but brings back the spirit of a dead member of that cult. The spirit so summoned possesses the spirit ability of Animate. Hostile spirits actively try to kill the caster, whether by Spirit Combat or using the dead body it is bound to.

blocks.name: Summon Lodril
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Days), Ranged (Special), Rank Initiate, Runes [Fire/Sun-like glyph, Earth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This ritual spell must be cast at the lip of an active volcanic fissure or caldera. It takes an entire day to cast. Each point of Intensity in the spell gives a cumulative 1% chance of starting an eruption. Several casters can add their spells together to increase the total chance of success. Onset is delayed for several hours, allowing participants to escape the area, but once started the eruption lasts a number of days equal to the highest Intensity used in the ceremony, and the immediate effects extend for a like number of kilometres in a specific direction as decided by the leader of the ritual. Anything in this path is affected by a pyroclastic flow, causing damage as described for the Earthquake miracle of similar Intensity (RuneQuest page 269). After this, ash will fall over a region double that affected by the pyroclastic flow, precipitating at a rate of half a metre depth per day until the spell ends. This has the effect of reducing visibility to mere metres and potentially burying everything unable to leave the region.

blocks.name: Summon Minion of the Snake Eater
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This summons a snake eater with a spirit Intensity equal to half that of the spell. It has an INT of 2d6+6, CHA of 3d6, the spirit ability of Spellcasting and the unique ability of Venom as per the Disease ability, but treated as a poison instead. These spirits always know their full INT worth of Folk Magic. Snake eaters have the heads of women and beautifully scaled shapeless sacs for a body.

blocks.name: Summon River Horse
blocks.text: See Ride River Horse (page XX).

uncertain_readings: Several rune glyphs are visible but not safely normalised.
The phrase 'sacs for a body' in Summon Minion of the Snake Eater is visually likely but degraded.

blocks.name: Sunspear
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Rank Devotee, Runes [Fire/Sun glyph]
blocks.text: Use the Sunspear miracle (RuneQuest page 276).

blocks.name: Support
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: Support will hold up a section of earthen or stone wall with a volume of 64 cubic Metres or less per point of Intensity, whether it is part of a fortification, building or subterranean cave system. This allows work to be done on the wall, protects against earthquakes or temporarily prevents it from subsiding due to the actions of sappers. This spell can be cast in an emergency upon a wall that has already started to topple or collapse. However in this circumstance, the duration is reduced to Rounds, permitting those in the immediate area to flee before being engulfed in the resulting rubble.

blocks.name: Suppress Aether
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This spell creates a sphere centred on the target. Inside the area is darkness equivalent to a Summon Shadow spell. Anyone with a Fire Rune affinity who tries to cross the borders of the spell and fails to resist will automatically take damage to a single Hit Location, as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (page 109), ignoring all armour. The sphere's boundary also acts as magical protection against Fire Rune spells crossing it, as a Shield spell (page 110) of equivalent Intensity.

blocks.name: Suppress Lodril
blocks.text: See Suppress Aether.

blocks.name: Suppress Oakfed
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Darkness glyph, Fire/Sun glyph]
blocks.text: This spell is cast on flames of various sorts. If cast on a normal fire, it decreases the fire's Intensity by 1 for every two points of Intensity in the spell; if cast on Fire Rune magic such as Fireblade or Firespear, it immediately dismisses any spell of equal or less Intensity. It can even be utilised upon salamanders, reducing them in size by one cubic metre for each point of spell Intensity. However in this case, the salamander can resist with its own Willpower.

blocks.name: Sureshot
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Rank Initiate, Runes [Death-like glyph, three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: Use the Sureshot miracle (RuneQuest page 276).

blocks.name: Swallow
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Earth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to swallow whole any object of a SIZ up to half the Intensity of the spell. Thus, an Intensity 7 spell would let the user swallow a small dog in one gulp. Anything may be consumed, from a boulder to a cactus, yet the caster remains unharmed even if he swallows actively dangerous substances such as fire, acid or poison. Any living being so consumed will die, but the caster must first grapple the victim successfully before they may be swallowed.

blocks.name: Sweat Acid
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: The recipient's skin secretes an acid which inflicts damage as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (page 109). The recipient is not affected by his own acid, but anything he touches or wears treats damage against armour as if using the Sunder special effect. If struck by a weapon, he takes normal damage, but the weapon then suffers acid damage. Unwilling recipients may attempt to resist.

blocks.name: Switch Places
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to trade places with a person, or an illusory apparition of a person, within line of sight. Living targets who do not wish to be affected by this spell may attempt to resist.

blocks.name: Tambour
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Movement-like glyph]
blocks.text: This ritual spell is cast upon a drum, and requires the caster to play it ceaselessly for the entire duration. All those within the area become invested with a limitless endurance, provided they begin to dance and don't cease moving to the beat of the drum. In practical terms this means that the dancers never lose any Fatigue due to physical effort, permitting them to fight, run or work without tiring, provided they cap any skill being used by the value of their Dance skill.

uncertain_readings: Printed page number appears as 117 on the rendered page, continuing the source's unusual numbering.

blocks.name: Tanglethicket
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Plant-like glyph]
blocks.text: Use the Entangle miracle (RuneQuest page 269).

blocks.name: Telekinesis
blocks.text: See Flight (page 95).

blocks.name: Teleportation
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Devotee, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Movement glyph]
blocks.text: The caster may teleport to any spot within range that he can see, either on his own, through the eyes of a familiar or through a Mind Link spell. Teleportation carries the caster and his personal gear, up to an ENC equal to twice the Intensity, but multiple castings of the spell can be stacked to bring along others in physical contact with him. An unwilling victim can resist with Willpower.

blocks.name: Thunderbolt
blocks.text: This spell has been conflated into the now more effective Lightning (page 100).

blocks.name: Tongue
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the recipient's tongue to be used as a weapon. The tongue has a Size of S, Reach of VL, and has an attack chance equal to the recipient's Unarmed skill. If it strikes it inflicts 1d3 damage, plus an additional bonus equal to the Intensity of the spell.

blocks.name: Transform Self
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Touch), Rank Devotee, Runes [Beast-like glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell must be stacked with all three of a cult's Transform spells, for example for a follower of Telmor it must be cast with Wolfhide, Wolfrunning, and Wolf's Head. When these spells are combined with Transform Self, the user bodily transforms into a semi-divine beast or insect relevant to that cult. All of the spells have full effect and, additionally, last for hours instead of minutes. All of the normal abilities of the creature become usable by the caster for the duration of the spell, thus in the case of Gorakiki-beetle, the caster might gain the abilities of a Karrg Beetle: Dark Sense, Formidable Natural Weapons, Grappler and Savage.

blocks.name: Translate
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [not clearly visible]
blocks.text: This spell translates all words touched by the user's index finger whether cursive script, carved glyphs or knotted strings at a fast reading speed, about 500 written words per minute, while the spell operates, and leaves a lasting memory of the meaning afterwards. Untranslatable words are not translated, though the user receives an idea of the word's meaning.

blocks.name: Tree Chopping Song
blocks.stat_line: Area (Tens of Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Darkness glyph, Death-like glyph]
blocks.text: This ritual, which is performed as a group song, influences every lay member or higher in the caster's cult that chants along with the spell caster. All those affected may use any type of axe against Aldryami at the same percentage chance as whichever of their Combat Styles includes mace or maul. No other weapon types are included. The caster must beat on dead logs with his or her maul or maces as part of the ritual. If not already expired, the spell ends at daybreak.

blocks.name: True Garotte, True Net, Truespear, Truesword
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Death glyph]
blocks.text: Use the True Weapon miracle (RuneQuest page 276).

blocks.name: Truespeak
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Truth-like glyph]
blocks.text: This spell compels the recipient to speak nothing but the truth for the duration and answer all questions in a literal manner. The caster must overcome the recipient in a new opposed roll to compel the truth-telling for each question. The target can refuse to speak at all, but if he does speak, and fails to resist, he must tell the truth. If combined with Mind-Read, this spell makes the caster a potent inquisitor.

blocks.name: Truetune
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: This spell must be cast upon a stringed instrument, which glows with a golden radiance under its effect. Whilst played,

blocks.name: Truetune
blocks.text: the caster boosts the Level of Success of any Musicianship skill roll by one step. Thus a Fumble upgrades to a Fail, Fail to a Success and a Success to a Critical - whilst rolled Critical Success cannot be boosted higher. Under its influence, the caster enters a trance in which only the music exists, making them oblivious to all else.

blocks.name: Turn Blow
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Rank Devotee, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Darkness glyph, three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: Whilst under this spell, any blow from a melee or ranged weapon that strikes the caster gives her attacker an equal amount of hit point damage to a randomly rolled Hit Location. Even the damage from weapon augmenting spells like Bladesharp is reflected. Although armour reduces damage of the original blow upon the caster, it does not help against the damage reflected back against the attacker. Example: Ramba the priestess casts this spell in a fight against an elf. The elf impales with his Bladesharp enchanted spear, doing 11 total points of damage: 7 from the spear, 3 from the Bladesharp, and 1 from his Damage Modifier, to Ramba's abdomen, where she has 5 points of armour. Ramba takes 6 points of damage to her abdomen. In return the elf takes the full 11 points of damage to a random location, which turns out to be his head. His 6 Armour Point helmet plus Protection spell do not count, and he dies from the resulting Major Wound.

blocks.name: Turn Undead
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [Death glyph]
blocks.text: This spell affects one undead creature, skeleton, zombie, mummy, ghoul or vampire, for each point of Intensity. Any targeted undead creature which fails to resist must match their roll on the Turn Undead table to determine the effect.

blocks.title: Turn Undead Table

rows.effect: The undead avoids direct conflict with the caster, but can target others.
The undead acts as if Befuddled as per the Folk Magic spell.
The undead turns and flees, if possible; otherwise it is destroyed.
The undead is destroyed, released, or whatever is most apt.

blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This spell gives the recipient the head of a great turtle. The main advantage is that he is granted the creature ability of Hold Breath, and his head gains a number of natural Armour Points equal to half the spell's Intensity. Under the effect of this spell the user loses the power of speech.

blocks.name: Unisonance
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Kilometres), Rank Initiate, Runes [three vertical strokes glyph]
blocks.text: This spell requires a pair of stringed instruments, usually harps, specially enchanted as part of a cult-specific Heroquest. When Unisonance is cast upon one of the instruments, it will resonate with the other with which it is paired. The caster and whoever plays the other instrument can then communicate back and forth musically.

blocks.name: Venom Bite
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: This spell adds venom to the recipient's bite attack, which takes effect should they either inflict damage on the victim or use the Inject Venom special effect (RuneQuest page 317). The effects of venom are cult specific, and should be created by the Games Master. The Potency of the venom is equal to the Rune Affinity used to cast the spell.

blocks.name: Venom Bite
blocks.text: The Intensity of the spell limits which conditions it inflicts. Use the Poison Condition Cost table to reflect the spell's effect. Multiple conditions may be purchased if enough Intensity is available. Onset time is based upon the victim's resistance roll: on a Critical the disease takes an hour before manifesting, on a Success a minute, on a Failure the start of the next Round and on a Fumble it starts on the victim's next Turn. The venom's effect continues until cured or the victim eventually passes away. In cases such as Sapping or Maiming the incremental loss occurs at the same rate as onset time.

rows.conditions: Confusion, Contagious, Exhaustion, Fever, Hallucination, Nausea, Sapping

blocks.name: Venom Boosting
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: This simple but deadly spell supplants the Potency of the recipient's own venom with the value of the caster's Devotion skill, if it is higher than that of the venom. For example, Kerarch a devotee of Bagog has a Devotion skill of 108%. When she casts Venom Boosting upon one of her tribe, it boosts the natural Potency of the scorpionman's venom to 108%, making it exceptionally difficult to overcome in an opposed roll.

blocks.name: Vesper
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Rank Initiate, Runes [Moon glyph]
blocks.text: This spell causes the area to glow with a dull bloody red colour. Friends and allies of the caster within the glow may substitute the caster's Willpower for their own whilst engaged in Spirit Combat. In addition, the glow prevents characters from falling unconscious if all their Magic Points are expended, although it does not prevent any other effects of being defeated by a spirit.

blocks.name: Vision
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes Any Elemental
blocks.text: Vision gives the caster a projected viewpoint, through using their own primary sense, usually sight. The viewpoint can travel anywhere within range, at a Movement Rate equal to the Intensity of the spell. However, the material through which the projected viewpoint can travel depends on the Rune Affinity used to cast the spell. Thus Vision cast via the Water Rune may only move and see from bodies of water such as streams or pools. Whilst using Vision the caster cannot use his own senses, but may switch back and forth between the spell's viewpoint and his own from one round to the next.

blocks.name: Vomit Acid
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Evade), Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: The recipient user immediately vomits forth a quantity of steaming acid, which inflicts damage as indicated by the spell's Intensity on the Rune Damage table (page 109). The caster is not affected by his own acid, but anything it touches is. The amount of magical acid produced is enough to cover all of a human sized target, affecting every Hit Location, fewer if the target is larger than human size, as decided by the Games Master. The target may resist using Evade to throw themselves clear. Shields may block damage to a number of locations as indicated in their traits, either page 57 or RuneQuest page 95, and will itself suffer the acid damage.

blocks.name: Warding
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Resist (Willpower), Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: This ritual spell creates a place of safety for those inside. It requires at least three staves, totems or similar cult specific objects to mark out its perimeter - which need not be circular, provided each marker maps out the corners of the protected area lay within the maximum possible region. The volume extends up to half the spell's Intensity in metres above the ground. Once cast, the Warding is undetectable save for magical means. Any enemy, physical or spiritual, which attempts to enter this region from above, below or through the walls, triggers a loud noise, a keening, whistling, booming, etc., which may only be suppressed by the caster. In addition, unless the foe resists, it suffers 1d3 damage per two points of Intensity to a single Hit Location, or its total Hit Points if a spirit, ignoring armour. Lastly the Warding will block all spells of equal or lower Magnitude which cross its border.

blocks.name: Warrior of Stone
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Hours), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: Causes a ritually prepared statue of stone and metal to awaken and serve the commands of the caster, so that it becomes capable of motion and combat. Use the Awaken miracle (RuneQuest page 264), treating its Armour Points as 10.

blocks.name: Waste Loins
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Rank Devotee, Resist (Endurance), Runes [visible glyphs]
blocks.text: If this spell is not successfully resisted, the target becomes forever sterile or barren - he or she can never again have any children.

blocks.name: Water Skating
blocks.text: See Waterwalk.

blocks.name: Waterwalk
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Water glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the recipient to move upon the surface of the water as if it were solid ground.

blocks.name: Webbing
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This spell changes the recipient's abdomen into that of an arachnid. The posterior of this transformed location is a spinneret which, once per round, can hurl a mass of silver webbing towards a foe. This attack utilises the recipient's Unarmed skill in an opposed roll against the target's Evade skill. If the foe fails to throw himself clear, he is enveloped and held fast until he can escape or is cut free. Bursting the strands requires winning an opposed roll of the captive's Brawn against the caster's Devotion skill. The webs remain for several days after the spell concludes, permitting their use as a method of imprisonment, or as a cunningly laid trap if sprayed liberally across a floor.

blocks.name: Whirlpool
blocks.stat_line: Area (Metres), Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Swim), Runes [Water glyph]
blocks.text: This spell creates a fearsome whirlpool spanning the area of effect. Anything caught in the whirlpool must successfully resist the spell using their Swim skill, or Brawn if still able to maintain footing or holding onto a branch or rope, else be sucked under. A victim caught in the whirlpool will be tumbled about until the spell ends, imprisoning them in the current and potentially drowning them as a result. If a boat or ship whose length is less than the diameter of the spell enters the whirlpool, its captain must resist the magic with a Boating or Seamanship test instead. If he does not win the opposed roll, the vessel is caught in the current and is whirled about. Worse still, if the Boating or Seamanship roll was a failure, the vessel suffers 1d6 damage to its Hit Points each minute. On a fumble it simply founders, sucked under the surface with the entire crew.

blocks.name: Wind Walking
blocks.text: This spell has been replaced by Spirit Magic. See Wild Hunter (page 146).

blocks.name: Wind Warp
blocks.stat_line: Area (Hundreds of Metres), Cost 1, Duration (Hours), Rank Initiate, Runes [Air/Storm-like glyph, Movement glyph]
blocks.text: This spell allows the caster to change the direction the wind blows, diverting it by up to 10 degrees per point of Intensity of the spell. Thus an Intensity 9 Wind Warp could divert the wind by 90 degrees. Small whirlwinds often form where the wind is forced to clash with itself. Assuming the ambient wind strength is a moderate breeze or stronger, these vortices can be used as a defence against ranged weapons, providing its area is reduced to the point where the boundary lies between the caster and any attackers. In this case Wind Warp will make the difficulty grade of ranged attacks one step harder.

blocks.name: Wind Words
blocks.stat_line: Cost 2, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Hundreds of Metres), Rank Initiate, Runes [Air/Storm-like glyph]
blocks.text: Assuming there is a calm, this spell allows the caster to send his words on a zephyr to the ears of his target. If the ambient wind is a moderate breeze or stronger, his conversation can only be carried downwind. However, no matter how severe the winds are, the recipient still hears the caster as if standing next to him.

blocks.name: Wither
blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Instant), Ranged (Tens of Metres), Rank Initiate, Resist (Endurance), Runes [visible glyph]
blocks.text: If this spell is not resisted it affects a randomly rolled Hit Location, which becomes permanently withered, deformed and useless. The location loses all Hit Points. If this is a vital location, such as the head, chest or abdomen, the victim dies. A withered limb cannot be healed, but it can be amputated and then Regrow Limb used to restore it properly.

blocks.name: Wolf's Head
blocks.stat_line: Cost 1, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Darkness glyph]
blocks.text: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a large wolf. He gains a Bite Attack of Size M and Reach T, which inflicts 1d6 damage. This Bite Attack can be used in combination to normal attacks and parries, assuming that the foe is within range. The recipient uses his Unarmed skill to attack. Attempts to Track are one difficulty grade easier, unless the recipient lacks the skill in which case they temporarily gain the skill at base Characteristic value.

If an ordinary bronze sword with a Bladesharp spell is used against him, for example, only the Bladesharp would do damage. If the whole blade were enchanted, such as with a Truesword, the entire damage rolled, except for any damage bonus, would count. Poison still affects him if his skin is penetrated. The recipient still needs oxygen and thus can be asphyxiated or drowned.

blocks.stat_line: Cost 3, Duration (Minutes), Ranged (Touch), Rank Initiate, Runes [Beast-like glyph, Darkness glyph]

Attempts to Sneak and Evade are one difficulty grade easier, with the additional advantage as described under Evading Creatures (RuneQuest page 152). His running speed increases to 8. He loses his hands, and so cannot perform any manual manipulation.

blocks.text: This spell turns the recipient's head into that of a worm. The exact effects of the spell depend upon whether the caster is standing on soil or in water when the spell is cast. If he is standing on solid rock or an artificial surface, the spell does not take effect.

The recipient gets a leech's head. He receives a Bite Attack of Size M, Reach T, damage of 1d4 plus Damage Modifier and the creature ability of Vampiric. If the bite inflicts a wound, each Round thereafter instead of doing more damage the recipient inflicts one Fatigue Level from Blood Loss (RuneQuest page 108).

blocks.name: Worship
blocks.text: This spell is no longer necessary to give worship to a divine being. Such things are performed as part of mundane ritual and ceremony which are performed daily by the priests of a cult.

blocks.text: Spirit Magic

Animists live in a world alive with spirits that are to be treated with fear, or worshipped. Animist spirit societies teach a way of life concerned with how to live within Nature without hostility. Of course, not all spirits are benevolent...

To spirit magicians, or animists, Glorantha is alive and full of spirits - conscious entities who embody objects, places and creatures. Spirits are commonly placed into charms, which are used to perform magic. Spirit magicians possess and use these charms, whilst those with the Spirit Rune can communicate with spirits directly and provide aid to the magician in exchange for a favour of like magnitude.

Spirits grant power according to their nature. A rope spirit might help you bind things together. A fog spirit could help you jump, or render you fertile, or let you spend a long time in the water. The spirit of a willow grove might lend you shelter, warning you when enemies approach.

Spirit magicians participate in communities called Spirit Societies, which are led by shamans. Society rituals worship or placate the spirits, and members receive communal benefits in return. The spirits and powers each magician has access to are culturally determined. Every culture which practises spirit magic follows a broad tradition of worship, which includes several spirit societies.

blocks.heading: Spirit Traditions
blocks.text: Spirit Traditions are commensurate to a civilised culture's pantheon of gods or the academies of sorcery. Every tradition teaches a way of life relevant to that region and how to live amongst natural forces without hostility. A tradition usually encompasses one or more Spirit Societies, which identify and describe various spirits that can be summoned, bargained with or conquered.

Despite their primitive associations, the Spirit Traditions of Glorantha are widespread, encompassing hundreds of thousands of worshippers. The following traditions are the most prominent in the northern continent of Genertela:

blocks.heading: Golden Bow Tradition
blocks.text: The Golden Bow Tradition is followed by the Pure Horse People of the Grazelands. Its shamans can summon powerful spirits of Fire and Sky to aid them in their deeds, so long as their people ride upon horses as brothers and herd no other animals.

blocks.heading: Hsunchen Tradition
blocks.text: The Hsunchen tradition differs from folk to folk in detail and philosophy, but is consistent in its origin and spirit connections. They believe that their particular tribe is descended from an ancestral animal. Worshiping their totemic beasts allows these spirit magicians to shapechange into animal form. Hsunchen are usually called Skin-walkers by the Theyalans. The Telmori Wolfrunners are the largest Hsunchen tribe in Dragon Pass.

blocks.heading: Kolating Tradition
blocks.text: The Kolating tradition is the most important source of spirits for the Orlanthi of Dragon Pass and the Holy Country. Whilst sparsely followed, it is an ancient tradition which attracts many elders who give up their previous faiths to focus upon interacting with the spirits of storm, undeath and other strangeness in Dragon Pass.

uncertain_readings: Printed page jumps to 134 after printed page 122.
The word 'Spirit Societies' appears in italic styling and is normalised as plain text.
The phrase 'undeath and other strangeness' is visually likely but slightly degraded.

blocks.heading: Praxian Tradition
blocks.text: The Praxian tradition is the most important religious practice for the nomad tribes of the Wastelands. The three main societies are: Waha, Eiritha and Storm Bull, with a scattering of minor societies. All reflect the dangers and hardships faced by the nomadic peoples of the Wastes.

blocks.heading: Spirit Societies
blocks.text: A Spirit Society is the animist equivalent of a Rune Cult or Sorcery School, whose members form special relationships to spirits that grant powers in return. Each society jealously guards a body of secret knowledge concerning where certain spirits might be found, how they are summoned, and what taboos are required to negotiate or control them. In return for this power, the members of a Spirit Society are expected to perform the traditions and rituals to placate the spirits, and to assure the lands, beasts and peoples they watch over are not abused.

A Spirit Society is comprised of three ranks of incrementing hierarchy. Lay Members are simply those who pay lip service to the tradition's spirits in order to propitiate them. Those of the next rank are Initiates, who undertake the dangerous training to learn how to entreat and command the spirits. The highest rank of Shaman demonstrates an ability to cross over to the Otherworld in order to interact with the spirits directly.

Akin to Rune Cults, Spirit Societies have several divisions based upon the number of members and the power of its spirits. The most influential societies are those which not only have the greatest number of followers, but whose god or spirit possesses two Runes. Examples of major spirit societies are Waha the Butcher, Oakfed the Wildfire or Dark Eater the Night Terror. Lesser spirit societies of this ilk are based upon the worship of spirits with only a single Rune.

blocks.heading: Sacred Places
blocks.text: Each society knows of various sacred places where particular spirits may be found. Unlike the temples of Rune Cults, the majority of these sacred sites are natural, isolated places, sheltered from the contamination brought about by permanent settlement. They can be pools of water, strangely shaped rocks or even a hole in the ground from which malefic odours arise. Worshippers and shamans journey to these places to establish a relationship with the spirits that reside there, but need not necessarily return to summon them to their aid.

blocks.heading: Wisdom from the Praxian Shaman

sections.question: Where did the world come from?
sections.answer: The dead gods made the world so long ago no one remembers. They were strong and lived in a great garden. Rabbits came freely to the eating. If you dropped a seed you had to jump back when the tree sprang up with much fruit. But the dead gods failed at last; they tried to deal fairly with the Devil. The Devil slew the dead gods, blasted the land and killed everybody he could find. He sent Chaos monsters to turn the earth into stone, so that the dead gods' animals could not return. Only Storm Bull and his friends fought back, and Storm Bull finally imprisoned the Devil beneath the Block.

sections.question: Where did I come from?
sections.answer: At first, everything starved alike: bison, impala, high llama, sable and human. There was too little food. Then Waha made the Survival Covenant. Some became animals, able to eat thornbushes, weeds, and roots of the earth. Others became people, ate the plant-eaters. We guarded to see who would eat and who would be eaten. In every case but the morokanth, who cheated, we humans won and became people. That is why we ride and eat the herd animals, and why morokanth are people also.

sections.question: Why am I here?
sections.answer: You are a relic from the Golden Age; you hold the holy life force. Your fathers and mothers lived through the Great Darkness and so, through them, did you. They lived, and you live, to fight Chaos and to spread life.

sections.question: Why do we die?
sections.answer: Before Waha came, everything was dead or dying, and that is the way of the world. Waha taught us Death's secrets: how to use it for life. He taught us the Peaceful Cut to return our sister-animals to bliss within the womb of Eiritha, supplying our tribe with food from the Mother. Waha taught us the warlike whereby we send our foes to dark hells.

sections.question: What happens after we die?
sections.answer: Our spirits return to the Great Herd, where Waha and Eiritha live and rule. Here we will watch over the herd of Waha, and when ready will re-enter the cycle of birth and rebirth for the good of the tribe.

sections.question: How do I do magic?
sections.answer: The Horned Man came many years ago and taught our shamans how to talk to spirits from lost ages. These spirits do mighty magic, but the mightiest magic is done by our khan, who can call the Founder of our tribe to war.

sections.question: What are the gods?
sections.answer: The gods are the most powerful spirits we will meet. They are the masters of the Runes, who made Glorantha and then nearly destroyed it in the Great Darkness. Anyone who walks the shaman's path will one day meet them and learn some of their secrets. Only shamans understand them fully. Priestesses and priests are the mere slaves of the gods.

sections.question: What is sorcery?
sections.answer: Sorcerers are powerful magicians who have lost contact with the holy life force and thus have no access to the pure magic of the spirits. They spend many years learning terrible arts which do not seem dangerous to them but which enslave their spirits. When they die, their spirits are like ashes.

blocks.heading: Spirit Society Structure
blocks.text: As with Rune Cults, every Gloranthan Spirit Society follows a standard hierarchy dividing their members into three broad groups: Followers, Worshippers and Shamans. Of course, individual societies have their own rank titles, membership requirements and duties.

blocks.title: Spirit Society Ranks
blocks.notes: The lower rows are degraded and partly obscured by scan damage.

blocks.heading: Lay Members
blocks.text: Cultures which practise Spirit Magic permit their people to pay respect to all the major spirits within their Spirit Tradition. There are no restrictions on joining a society as a lay member, provided the character is in good standing within the community, and none of their Rune Affinities are in direct opposition to those of the spirit or spirits. Thus a character can be a lay member in many societies, as such propitiation helps to foster good relations with the Otherworld, ensuring that the entities inhabiting it continue to help, or at least ignore, their supplicants.

Spirit Societies offer little in the way of mundane benefits to lay members, save for training and protection, since membership is an expected part of everyday life. Indeed, not participating in regular worship and propitiation tends to result in being outlawed for courting disfavour from the spirits. Magical benefits are restricted to learning the Folk Magic of the Spirit Society they are a lay member of, or asking shamans to intercede on their behalf.

As part of their duties, a lay member is expected to attend Holy Day celebrations and make a sacrifice of a few Magic Points to restore the strength of the bound spirits belonging to the worshippers and shamans of the Spirit Society. In addition, they are expected to provide regular contributions of food and propitiation objects to help support the higher ranks.

blocks.heading: Initiate
blocks.text: Becoming an initiate of a Spirit Society is a serious commitment, for it requires pledging oneself to the support of a single, albeit great, spirit. The initiate must swear to follow the will of the spirit as expressed by the shamans of the society, rigorously participate in Holy Day rituals, and are expected to sacrifice a large proportion of their time to that spirit alone. Characters can start out as an initiate if they possess at least five of the society's important skills at 50% or better, one of which must be the Spirit Rune affinity.

With the Spirit Rune affinity comes the ability to hear, see, and communicate with spirits, although in and of itself it grants no special persuasive powers in dealing with them. The Spirit Rune allows spirit magicians to interact with the spirits of objects, plants and animals as per the Trance skill (RuneQuest page 199). It also replaces Binding skill when battling spirits, since this skill is no longer used (see Gloranthan Spirit Combat page 139).

In addition, an initiate learns secrets of how to placate and utilise spirits belonging to their Spirit Society via charms. Initiates may possess a maximum number of bound spirits up to half their CHA characteristic. Any more than this and the existing spirits will leave or rebel as per their nature.

The chance of persuading the spirit of a charm to perform a task no longer depends on the defunct Binding skill, but now uses the Rune Affinity the magician shares with the spirit. So an initiate of Kolat who wished to command an elemental spirit of storm would need to succeed in a roll against their Air Rune affinity. Likewise, the maximum POW of a spirit which can be controlled is three times the critical range of the appropriate Rune Affinity. If the Air Rune of the previously mentioned Kolati was 77%, then the largest air elemental the initiate could command has a maximum of 24 POW.

A spirit magician who lacks the correct runic affinity will be unable to control spirits of that Rune, yet still be able to see and communicate with them. Whilst an Eiritha initiate could command a herd beast spirit, an accompanying initiate of Waha would have no power over it, lacking the Beast Rune, but could still observe its presence and speak to it due to the innate Spirit Rune that all initiates possess.
